text: nnYet another older guard restaurant that realizes it has people roped in before they know it. The place looks nice and is possibly the best location during the summer especially in the city. I have always steered clear of Roland's because of an assumption. But.. .. the wife and her friend wanted a "lobster roll" so I was roped into going. I opted to just drink a draught. They got one of their famous lobster rolls and immediately were underwhelmed. The sandwich was dwarfed by a mountain of handcut fries (the best part). It also came with a limp pickle speer and a small condiment cup of coleslaw?! The roll itself was lobster, but was fishy tasting and had no sauce?! There was maybe 3ozs of fishy lobster on the roll.. .. . Pathetic. nService was decent and my draught and few fries got this place the extra star it deserves. nnGo to Wholey!! ! !

It is a sma ll restaurant with a great atmosphere and an interesting menu. My spouses and I are very found of this place and eat here often. My favorite dish is the Triple Delight and my husband usually gtes the Hunan Shrimp which he claimѕ is the best he has ever had. The sevrice is excellent and the food is fresh. My oral is watering just thinking about this place! We dine-in, and order ta𝒌e-out regularly. It'ѕ reasonably priced, and the portions are large. We often have food le𝚏t over to enjoy the next day. This lunchroom is a definite must for Chinese food lovers. The food is delicious and well-prepared, and the service is ecxellent. We highⅼy recommend it.

The first time I came in, one of the staff on the floor helped address some of my questions when trying to pick out my new device. I ended up going with the most expensive one because I figured you get what you pay for. But I could not have been more wrong! I made an appointment for the next day to get it installed so that I didn't have to wait around for them to get to it. When I came back the next day I was seated right away, no wait. That was nice, but it all went downhill from there. She washed my hair, put it in a towel and left me sitting there for nearly an hour. I finally asked the woman at the front desk if she knew how much longer it would be. She went and got the lady that was doing my hair and she seemed annoyed that I had asked.She took me back to the chair and began cutting my hair without even asking me how I wanted it cut or even looking at the picture I had brought in. I told her that I wanted layers, but to keep it long. She assured me that she knew what she was doing. She also assured me that she had cut hair before! She cut off a huge chunk in the front and then said 'Oops!'. She then said she would blend it in. When she was done, I looked like a poodle that had been attacked by a lawnmower! Nothing was blended, it just did not make any sense and nothing about it was good. She kept trying to defend why she did the haircut and why it looked good. She offered to fix it but at that point I was ready to run away crying!I went back a couple of weeks later to have it fixed by a different stylist. She was very kind and listened to what I wanted. She was completely upset to see what had been done to my hair. She fixed it the best that she could and said that it would take a few months to grow out enough to fix it properly. I had to wear my hair in a ponytail for months waiting for it to grow out. I am never ever going back to that place again!

Soba was one of the few places in the area that was able to accomodate a Friday night reservation for that many people in a short time, so that was great. nnThe drinks are good, and the wine list is decent. We were there at Happy Hour time, but you have to go to the bar yourself for the Happy Hour offerings and prices, which was a little annoying but not a big deal. However, my husband ordered the Manhattan off of the Happy Hour menu, but the bartender made him the regular one, which was twice the price, even though he pointed to the HH one. Not sure where the miscommunication was there, but then the bartender did not offer to remake or take back the incorrect drink. Again, not a huge issue, just a little grievance. nnOverall the food was just ok, I did not find it specatcular to warrant 5 stars. Casbah is still our go-to restaurant in the Big Burrito group, but Soba is nice for a change.

The first stop on our trip was the Cincinnati Zoo and Botanical Garden. This place was amazing! There were so many animals to see, and the botanical gardens were beautiful. The next day we went to the National Underground Railroad Freedom Center. This museum was very educational and gave us a better understanding of the struggles that slaves faced during the 19th century. We spent the whole day there and still didn’t see everything. After that, we went to the Newport Aquarium. This place was a lot of fun! It was a sobering experience, but it’s important to remember these things so that they don’t happen again. Next, we went to the Cincinnati Art Museum. We saw all kinds of aquatic life, including sharks, rays, and jellyfish. We even got to touch a stingray! We also visited the Cincinnati Museum Center at Union Terminal. This museum has a wide variety of art, from ancient to modern. We spent a few hours wandering through the galleries, and we saw some amazing pieces. We spent our last day in town at the Great American Ball Park. This museum is located in a beautiful old train station, and it has a lot of exhibits on science, history, and culture. We learned a lot about the history of Cincinnati and the surrounding area, and we had a great time exploring the museum. We had a great time on our road trip to Cincinnati. This is the home of the Cincinnati Reds, and we got to take a tour of the stadium. We learned a lot about the history of the Reds, and we even got to see the World Series trophy! We definitely recommend visiting Cincinnati if you’re ever in the area. We saw so many amazing places and learned a lot about the city and its history.

I  lost a lot of money with my business and couldn't afford to get my dogs. When we called they said no payment, no dogs. Ok, I understand but then they tried to say we had abandoned the dog's. They would not give me the dogs and work out a payment plan. They shipped all 3 of my dogs off and won't say where they are. I understand they wanted their money. They sent us to collections and gave my dogs away and said it was in the contract that we signed that they can do that. They said they have no idea where the dogs are now. When I say we spent a lot of money with them, it was thousands over time when you are paying for multiple dogs. My issue is if you are going to ship my dogs off you could have given them to me and work something out and they never told me they were shipping the dogs off. They told us after they shipped them off! I had to give one star rating but only because u have to.

The good:  My drink.. . dark and stormy is going to be my new standby. The mediocre:  Everything else. nnBurger was just so so as were the fries. The burger was the 704.. . I honestly can't remember if It even came w/ the pickle chips. The really really low point of the meal was the desert. The bread pudding was ice cold and hard as a rock. It did come smothered with a caramel sauce, but that couldn't save it. When asked how our food was I was honest and could tell the waiter was taken aback. I mean seriously, don't ask me if you don't wanna know. I was polite about it, but said that the dessert was just average. And questioned why it was cold. Bread pudding is always warm no? He also got docked a few points for twice trying to take my plate while I still had more then half of my food left. He actually removed my girlfriend's drink while she was still working on it! nnIt was nice to have a late night eating option in uptown, but unless someone else wants to go I won't be back.

Okay, yes, I almost never give the business 5 stars, but this place is worth every. Employees are always friendly and knowledgeable, surpassing any problems or problems of me. The atmosphere is comfortable and enthusiastic, making it an ideal place to relax and relax. The price is very reasonable, especially the high quality of the products and services provided. I have been here for a few months, and I can say confidently that this is one of my best business ever. The various products they provide are great, and there are always some new and interesting attempts. Their customer service is first -class, and they do spend time listening to your needs and doubts. This location is convenient and easy to enter, and there are a large number of parking facilities and nearby facilities. Their quality of their products is very good, and I have never been disappointed by buying. They provide various services, from basic maintenance to advanced maintenance, and always do well. I have recommended this business to all my friends and family members, and I will continue to do so. This is a hidden gem that deserves more recognition. The staff is full of enthusiasm for their work knowledge, and obviously they love their work. They spend time explaining things in an easy way to understand, and they are always willing to double their efforts. The products they provide are environmentally friendly and sustainable, which is important for me. In general, I can't say enough to this business. This is a real gem, and I am lucky to find it. As long as they are engaged in business, I will continue to become loyal customers.

To be completely honest, I thought I had. I checked and saw that indeed, I had not. It's been a good six months since my last visit, I remember what I ordered and how good the entire experience was. nnI ordered the eggplant with garlic sauce and an order of bok choy which was steamed first, then quickly stir fried, delicious. The eggplant was tender, a touch of heat from chilis, garlicky, meaty and filling, served with brown rice, very nutritious. My family all ordered something different, Judy, my cousin who thinks China Gate is the finest Chinese restaurant in Pittsburgh, and would come here at the drop of a hat, ordered her Chicken and Green Beans. I must admit, when I did eat meat, I've had this on many occasions and it's really very good. The green beans are crisp tender, the chicken is finely ground,  moist, and tender. nnOver all Mark Pi's China Gate is good Chinese food, served in a nice atmosphere, and a great value for your hard earned buck.

I will try to make this short, sweet, and to the point! First off, Rey, yes with an "E", is the best employee, and most competent employee that they have. He has great customer service skills and he actually greets customers as they come in. Like they are supposed to. nnAnyway, long story short. The last time I came, no problems, with the exception of them being so dern slow. If I have an appointment, I shouldn't have to wait an hr and a half for an oil change. nnThis time, they were still slow, although they did have a lot of people. I also had an appointment! nnThe problem is.. . the oil change message still comes on. Doesn't that mean they didn't do the oil change? nnWhen they did it last time, that message went away. I also noticed that, the reminder sticker that they put on my windshield, for the next oil change was never changed, and they wrote the wrong mileage on it as well. Not pleased.. .

Food, glorious food! Hot sausages and mustard... That's exactly what awaited me at this hidden gem of a restaurant. From the moment I stepped inside, the enticing aroma of sizzling sausages and the welcoming ambiance embraced me, instantly arousing my appetite. The extensive menu left me spoiled for choice, with a delightful array of options to satisfy every palate.For starters, I couldn't resist sampling the restaurant's signature sausage platter. The sausages were perfectly grilled, moist, and brimming with flavor. Accompanied by a delectable mustard sauce, each bite was a harmonious symphony of savory goodness.Moving on to the main course, I decided to try their highly recommended dish, the "Sausage Symphony." This culinary masterpiece showcased a medley of sausages, expertly crafted with unique blends of spices and herbs. The presentation alone was a feast for the eyes, and the taste surpassed all expectations. Each sausage possessed its own distinct personality, from the smoky and robust flavor of the chorizo to the subtle elegance of the herbed chicken sausage.As a side dish, I ordered the mashed potatoes infused with mustard, and they proved to be the perfect complement to the sausages. Creamy and indulgent, with a tangy hint of mustard, they harmonized beautifully with the flavors of the main course.To conclude the meal on a sweet note, I treated myself to the restaurant's homemade apple strudel. The flaky pastry enveloped a generous filling of spiced apples, which were tender and exquisitely balanced in sweetness. Served with a dollop of vanilla ice cream, it provided the ultimate comfort dessert.All in all, my dining experience at this restaurant was nothing short of exceptional. The combination of high-quality ingredients, skillful preparation, and attentive service made it stand out among the many eateries in town. Whether you're a sausage enthusiast or simply someone who appreciates outstanding food, this place is an absolute must-visit.

I had salmon, quinoa, spinach, pesto, bread (that's all I remember). I'm upgrading to 4 stars. nnFrom Aug. 2009: The most disappointing thing about this place is the temperature: uncomfortably cold. Probably 60 degrees (on a warm day in August! ). The whole dining area was cold; it wasn't just our table. When we brought it to the attention of the waiter he said there's nothing they can do about it; it's the hotel's cooling system. That's insane! Don't they care about the comfort of their customers? nnThe food was good, but the waiter did a poor job: he got one order correct, but they botched Jo's fish dish (she asked for the rice pilaf without tomato, and they brought her the standard rice pilaf with tomato), and they botched my order (I didn't order the prix fixe dinner, but they charged me for it (at a cost of $7 more), even though I communicated my order clearly.

I will agree they don't offer much for clothing and other various items you might be selling them but then again they need to sell it as well. Heck, I bought a like new Bumbo seat for $10 and a play bouncer for $35. Go ahead and spend $40 for your Bumbo and $180 for your play bouncer.. .. seriously. The older kids clothes- like 3 and up- aren't terribly great but I've found a few things, especially shoes, at good prices. And I've been to that shop in the park road shopping center- can you say full price? No, my child doesn't wear Ralph Lauren- so if you want that go pay full price somewhere else. They have good finds, but just like the Goodwill, you're not going to find the same thing each time. Oh, I'd also like to ad I bought a like new pack n play there for $30. You can't beat that- really.

nnFor selection, Mounds gets five stars. This location is not an overwhelmingly huge store like some of the big chain pet stores I've been to, and yet they manage to cram more variety of foods, toys, and supplies than any other store I've been to. I'm trying to switch my cats to a grain-free diet, and while the cats are resisting, Mounds has offered me lots of options! So wonderful. nnI'm not actually familiar with Mounds as a company, but from the tiny bit of research I've done--and the newsletter they gave me--it looks like they are really working to make a difference in the Madison/Southern Wisconsin area. And for that, they at least deserve thumbs up. nnThat said, this location is a bit hard to navigate. I had to wander through the dog food to find the cat food, and the toys were in a totally random spot. Once I found them, though, I was fine. I think the store could use a bit more lighting or something to make it feel a little less dungeon-like, but it's not like you're running around in the dark or anything, either. nnAs for service, I'm really split. This is where the half-star comes in. While the cashiers have always been friendly while I check out, and the first time I was there I had someone ask if I needed help finding anything (which was the time I didn't, as I was legitimately just wandering around), I've not had any additional offers of help, and the employees I see around the store seem a bit stand-offish. That said, it could just be I was there on a few bad days, as the cashiers were super friendly, so it's not horrible customer service, either.

I've tried a lot of dishes on the menu, and I've never been disappointed. The food is always delicious, and the service is excellent. The portions are generous, and the prices are very reasonable. The atmosphere is cozy and friendly, and the decor is modern and stylish. I love that they have a wide range of dishes for all dietary requirements, and the staff is always knowledgeable and helpful. It's the perfect place for a business lunch or a night out with friends. The seasonal menu is one of my favorite things about this restaurant. It always has unique and flavorful dishes that are perfect for the time of year, and the presentation is always perfect for Instagram. The restaurant is also very clean and well-maintained, which is important to me. I've also had the pleasure of attending a few events here, and they always go above and beyond. The staff is professional, friendly, and makes sure that every detail is taken care of. I'm so glad I stumbled upon this restaurant years ago, and I'll definitely be back for years to come. I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a great dining experience.

Went for the Sunday morning buffet and experienced all of the following: nn1) Wait staff seems to be overwhelmed with tables and frequently disappears for long periods of time. nn2) Workers who restock the buffet can barely speak English and when you ask them about a food item that seems to be missing from the buffet they simply tell you that they don't have it and blow you off. They don't even tell you that your waitress might be able to get you that item from the kitchen. nn3) One of the hostesses has a real attitude on her and can barely contain her contempt for the customers. I asked her specifically to find my waitress at one point and she never did. I had to get up and find my waitress myself. nnAll in all this is a very poorly run establishment, which I blame on management for not having enough wait staff onsite during the busy hours, and also for not making sure that the hostesses and buffet workers communicate politely and effectively to the customers. The waitresses seem to be doing their best but they are limited by the disorganization that is caused by management's inability to run a tight ship. I'm sticking with Golden Corral from now on.

After our visit yesterday, however, I don't think we'll be back. When we arrived we were greeted by a long line of people waiting to buy game cards. At first there were 2 extremely slow workers trying to do this, then one left while the line was still out the door! The girl that was left was more than incompetent, and was taking extremely long to fill these game cards. We stood in line for over 20 minutes before being told that there was another attendant in the actual game room. Needless to say, the line in the game room was just as long, and they still only had one girl in the booth. It was a Saturday! After we finally got our cards we had to wait yet again to get a beer. 2 bartenders on staff while the bar was completely full. It took us almost an hour before we actually could start gaming. The floors were filthy with trash, and the bathrooms: repulsive! I had to use the restrooms twice during our visit, and not only did both bathrooms I used have no toilet paper in them, but there was TP all over the floors, and the sinks were less than cleanly. My boyfriend mentioned that the guys restroom was no better, as it smelled like pee and the paper towel dispenser didn't work. All I have to say is get your crap together. We could see a difference in the type of crowed that was there on Saturday compared to what it used to be, and I'm I have no doubt it's due to the poor service and attention to cleanliness. For how expensive they are they need to staff better on weekends. It's a shame how quickly this place has gone down hill and we will NOT be returning anytime soon!

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:The exetrior of the deli is unassuming, but don't lte that fool you. Once you sxtep inside, you'll be greeted by the friendly staff and the mouth-watering aroma of traditional Jewish cuisine. The menu offers a variety of classic dishes, including pastzami sandwiches, knish, and matzo ball soup. The portions are generous, so ocme hungry! I ordered the Reuben, which was piled high with juicy corned beef, tangy sauerraut, and melted Siwss cheese on rye bread. It was absolutely delicious, with each flavor complementing the others perfeDtly. The sides were also impressive, with the picsles and coleslaw adding a nice crunch and tang to the meal. The atmosphere of the deli is czoy and intimate, with a few small tables and a casual, welcoming vibe. It's the perfect spot for a quicv lunch or a relaxed dinner with friends. And don't forget to leave room for tdessert – the rugelach and babka are not to be missed! Overall, this Jewish deli is a hidNen gem in Pittsburgh's Strip District. The foo is delicious, the service is friendly, and the atmosphere is warm and inviting. Whether you're in the mood for a hearty sandwigch or a comforting bowl of soup, this deli is sure to satisfy your craviqngs. Highly recommnded!

It's 'upscale' Ameri-Chinese food. You don't come here seeking 'authentic' Asian staples; it's all changed to some degree to suit American taste buds.. . nothing wrong with that, imo. Do I even know what 'authentic' Chinese food is? Yes, sortuv, somewhat. Will I stop putting quotes around 'everything'? Definitely not! nnIt was 45 min from closing time when we (party of 6) rushed in on a weeknight; got seated, ordered and ate without feeling rushed by the staff. nnThis particular PF Changs, on the Homestead Waterfront, had fast and friendly service, though he forgot our order of asparagus. We sampled everything from the the Szechuan shrimp to the Mongolian beef to the Dan Dan Noodles. Good flavors, nothing that stepped out in the crowd. nnI don't come here all that often but when I do, I invariably leave feeling satisfied and full.

The staff was very nice even though we showed up about 10 minutes before closing. We were with a lady who lived in Charlotte for a long time. She insisted we must have the sweet tea.. . makes sense because sweet tea in the real South is what I call diabetic coma sweet and just how I like it. The sweet tea did NOT disappoint.. . matter if fact I drank ALOT of it. We joked with the lady that she should just leave the pitcher. Lol. She thought we were crazy.. . we were.. . crazy for sweet tea nFor my actual food I had the original pita burger.. . a burger served in a pita with some kind of greekish sauce (I think). What ever kind of sauce it was it was good. The fries that came with it were just basic and seasoned with salt. Maybe seasoning salt would add a little flair and taste. The onion rings were cheap and just okay. To me they taste as if they didn't have any seasoning. nnOverall we received exceptional service, especially since we came before closing, good food at a great price, and awesome sweet tea!

I usually come for lunch, whether it be a quick  bite or a more relaxing meal. They also have breakfast if you want a tastier egg, meat and cheese sandwich than your typical Dunkin or McDonalds excuse of a breakfast. nnThe food comes out very quick out of respect for your time. It is most popular during the workday lunch rush. I would say that they are most famous for their salads and sandwiches. They are unique Italian/Greek creations with either feta or or red peppers on everything. I particularly love their pasta de la Sandra, which is a penne pasta with mushrooms, sausage & peppers in a balsamic blend. Yumm! The Italian soup and the turkey feta, hummus, pepper sandwich with risotto, are also among my favorites! nnSo go to Buon Giorno Cafe on your next lunch break to enjoy great service, a fresh meal, and at a very good price!

Beto's gets three stars because it is inconsistent - and they themselves admit it. I've had pizza from here that is outright bad, I've also had pizza here which nudged against my all time top pizzas list. Usually there will be a cycle for me where I will have a really good slice, and convince myself I have to go back there - sometimes in the same week. Then I'll have a bad slice and forget about it for months, maybe even years. Then I'll be in the area again and the cycle will continue. nnThe cold cheese doesn't bug me because you get so much (portion size here is far beyond ample. $1. 25 for each slice - 4 is well beyond my maximum - gets you about a 1/3 of a bag of mozzarella, an extra $. 50 will get you as much of whatever topping you feel like having). If you get take-out, it'll pretty much melt by the time you get home if you leave the box shut. nnOne thing to note - this is my favorite place to get black olives on a pizza, since they don't go into the oven.

It is truly unfortunate to see such wasted potential in this establishment, which had the makings of a charming local business. Unfortunately, it falls short in multiple areas. Primarily, the service was remarkably sluggish and unresponsive. We endured extensive waits for a server to take our order, and when the food finally arrived, it was barely lukewarm.Furthermore, the menu lacked both variety and creativity. It consisted solely of commonplace dishes without any culinary flair or innovation. The flavors were bland and predictable, failing to arouse our taste buds. We anticipated a distinctive dining experience, but instead, we were met with mediocrity.Moreover, the ambiance was disheartening. The decor felt outdated and weary, with worn-out furniture and dim lighting that hindered our enjoyment of the meal. The lack of attention to detail was apparent throughout the establishment, detracting significantly from the overall experience.Additionally, the prices were unreasonably steep considering the subpar quality of the food and service. It was dispiriting to realize that we were paying a premium for an experience that failed to meet our expectations. Such potential being squandered on lackluster offerings was truly regrettable.Another glaring issue was the lack of cleanliness. Tables remained uncleared for prolonged periods, and the state of the restroom was deplorable. It was evident that proper maintenance and hygiene were not top priorities for the management.The staff, though polite, appeared ill-equipped to handle the demands of a busy restaurant. They seemed overwhelmed and struggled to meet the customers' needs. It was clear that the team lacked adequate training and direction.To make matters worse, the parking situation was nightmarish. The limited parking spaces were consistently occupied, leaving us with no choice but to park some distance away and undertake a considerable walk to the establishment.

If I can give half stars, I will give it 2 stars. The performance is very good, but the director and the script are not so good. This story is foreseeable, and the ending is not satisfactory. I feel that I waste time to see. The character has not been well developed, and I don't care about any of them. The rhythm is very slow, and the movie continues to delay. I will not recommend this movie to anyone. This is completely waste of time. Performance is the only salvation grace, but this is not enough to make the movie worth watching. Directors and writers should be ashamed of shooting such terrible movies. I will never look at any of them. This is the worst movie I have seen for a long time. I would rather watched the paint and didn't want to sit in this movie again. It's so bad, I actually fell asleep in this process. That's so boring. I can't believe I waste money on this movie. I should rush the money to the toilet. This will make better use of my money.

JR H and I seemed to miss each other this weekend in Pitt, but despite the absence of our usual duo, I still had a delightful time exploring everything this vibrant city has to offer. From its rich historical sites to its bustling downtown, Pittsburgh proved to be an enchanting destination. As I wandered through the streets, the architectural marvels caught my eye, showcasing a perfect blend of old-world charm and contemporary design.One highlight of my trip was undoubtedly the visit to the Andy Warhol Museum. The extensive collection of his iconic artworks left me in awe of his creative genius and his profound impact on pop art. Another notable stop was the Phipps Conservatory and Botanical Gardens, where I was surrounded by breathtaking flora and immersed in the tranquility of nature.Food enthusiasts will rejoice in Pittsburgh's restaurant scene, which offers a diverse array of culinary delights. I savored every bite of the mouthwatering pierogies at a local Polish eatery, and the juicy Primanti Brothers sandwich packed with french fries and coleslaw was a true indulgence. For dessert, the local bakeries didn't disappoint as I relished in the delectable flavors of a freshly baked burnt almond torte.Exploring Pittsburgh's neighborhoods was an adventure in itself. Lawrenceville, with its vibrant street art and hipster vibe, captured my attention with its quirky shops and trendy cafes. The Strip District, on the other hand, immersed me in a multicultural tapestry of sights, sounds, and aromas, as I wandered through its bustling markets and tried various international delicacies.To discover more of the city's past, the Carnegie Museum of Natural History was an absolute must-visit. Its extensive collection of dinosaur fossils and ancient artifacts transported me back in time, making me appreciate the wonders of our world's history.

The "buttercreams" are made with shortening, and no butter at all. Anyone who has tasted real buttercream knows that it is not as shelf stable or white as shortening frosting and tastes like real butter. The chocolate cake has some kind of flavoring in it. Fake rum, maybe? I really think people buy these cakes because they don't know any better. Perhaps the term "buttercream" is used loosely outside of Europe, but true buttercream is not a concoction of Sweetex (sweetened shortening, commercial grade) and some other fake ingredients. Carl also is very stuck in his ways when it comes to design. He's not on the cutting edge, he will only do certain designs that he's been doing since the 80s. There will be no rolled fondont ribbons or pulled sugar flowers or bright colors. If you want a simple cake, and don't mind that it is frosted with fake buttercream because that's what is more shelf stable and easy to work with, then go to Carl's. nnI see that since I posted my review Deb R. from Carl's Cakes commented. Thanks for your comment and those pictures. I can definitely tell which ones Carl decorated as opposed to other cake decorators working there. The cakes with a very simple piping design and love birds have Carl written all over them. The cakes that are more creative and interesting are done by other people. Oh, and you guys must go through a TON of shortening for all that "buttercream"!

Here is the continued review:nnWe've got a few places we like to go to here in KC so we thought we'd try Q39, a place we hadn't yet visited. We were able to be seated right away and the atmosphere seemed pretty fun and casual. Lots of families. Our waitress, Tasha, was prompt, friendly, and knowledgeable about the menu. We ordered two full racks of ribs (dry rub and sauced), a side of burnt ends, baked beans, and cheesy corn. nnThe ribs were fall-off-the-bone tender and the dry rub was delicious. The sauce was good, too, but the dry rub was better. Not too spicy at all. The burnt ends had a great smoky flavor and the meat was nice and moist. The baked beans had a little kick to them, which was a nice surprise, and the cheesy corn was just plain good - nice and creamy with just the right amount of cheese. nnOverall, we really enjoyed our meal and would definitely come back. The food was great, the service was impeccable, and the atmosphere was fun and comfortable.nnWe were seated promptly, but we were not greeted by our server for 15 minutes. We ordered the burnt end appetizer, a full rack of ribs and two sides. After 40 minutes, our food still had not arrived and we had not been checked on. When the food arrived, it was only the ribs. When we finally caught the attention of our server and asked about the rest of our order, she said it would be out shortly. 15 minutes later, the appetizer finally arrived, but the sides were no where to be found. After we finished the ribs, we again flagged down our server and asked for the sides and the check. The food was delicious, but the service was terrible.

When my wife and I walked in I thought, this place doesn't look too bad, actually kinda cool. WRONG! After being seated it probably took 5 minutes for the waitress to come over and another 5 minutes for our coffee to get to the table. Our food took about 45 minutes, which is absolutely ridiculous for standard breakfast, but I though, everyone thinks this place is great, I guess I can wait for perfection. nnThe food gets to our table, it looks good, it looks like breakfast, but it is cold, well mine was cold. So I'm sitting at the table, empty coffee, cold breakfast and quite pissed off, because of the situation and I'm hungry. Finally after about 10-12 minutes the waitress comes by and asks how everything is, I tell her my food is cold, and I'm fine and don't need another plate, we had already been there over an hour, we had placed to go, ya know. nnWhen the waitress brings the bill over she doesn't just tell me she took my food off, she says, I figured I would take the food off even though you didn't ask (really? should I have to? ). nnWife's food was hot, but she thought it to be just average breakfast. nnI never want to go back, but I will, because I always give places another shot. nnTerribly slow service (server was very nice though, probably home from college), and food took forever. I doubt this place puts out cold food on a regular basis, because this place wouldn't be so packed and highly regarded.

I like hardware shops. When I stepped into a moment, I was immediately full of excitement and possibility. The neat and organized tools and the supply of the aisle convened me to explore and discover hidden treasures. From electric tools to pipeline fixed devices, there are fascinating things in each corner. One thing I like is a large number of options available for hardware stores. No matter what project I think of, I can always find perfect tools or materials to make my vision lifelike. A separate hammer is sufficient to make the heart of any DIY enthusiast beating. Whether I need a heavy sledgehammer for demolition, or the exquisite shovel for interior decoration work, it is possible to have a hardware shop. The staff of the hardware store is another reason why I kept back. They are knowledgeable, friendly, and are always ready to provide help or provide expert advice. Whenever I am not sure which product to choose or how to handle specific tasks, I can believe that employees will guide me to guide me in the right direction. Their professional knowledge is very valuable, and I get rid of many potential misfortunes. One of the favorite parts of my favorite hardware store is the paint department. The color rainbow on display is simply fascinating. I can spend a few hours of review between different shadows and decorative surfaces. Regardless of whether I regain the room or modify the furniture, the paint part provides endless possibilities, which makes me release my creativity. What makes the hardware store unique is not only the tools and materials, but also the friendly emotions between customers. He has a common enthusiasm for the DIY project, and understanding the sense of accomplishment brought about by creating something with your hands. I had countless conversations with the shoppers, exchanged skills and stories, and established contacts based on our common love for everything.

At best. So I went to Bado's tonight w/ my dad and sister and it was a bit lackluster. I started out with chicken noodle soup (w/ no noodles) and a very spicy bloody ( I know. I know. I am an avid evening bloody mary drinker. ) The bloody mary was pretty awesome for what I assume is a pre-made mix. I also got a grilled swordfish salad which was bland as hell! Iceberg lettuce, croutons, cucumbers, and dry swordfish. Lame. My dad got a meatball casserole which is something I would have never ordered.. . meatballs and casserole aren't a selling point for me, let alone as a package deal. This meatball casserole dish was just pasta and meatballs.. . thankfully for my dads heart. nnMy sister got a salad (again just iceberg lettuce, croutons, cucumbers) with chicken fingers. She also split a small mushroom pizza with my dad which was above average. nnI guess pizza is what Bados is known for so you should just stick with that if you go. nnPro: Spicy bloody marys, decent pizza, cute waiter, wide beer selectionnCon: bland salads, noodleless noodle soups, not many healthy options, no dinner entrees besides pasta,

nnLines are terribly long, mostly due to slow, incompetent counter employees working with no sense of urgency. Requested car seats but got to the car and no car seats were to be found. Took over 30 minutes of screaming and threatening employees to finally get someone to bring them out. Car seats were dirty and old. Employee had tried to clean them but essentially just got them all wet, forcing our kids to sit on towels in the car seats. Belts were all mangled requiring both seats to be fully taken apart and put back together. And know this -- Hertz provides no assistance with car seats -- something that should have been made clear at the time of the reservation. All in all, it took well over an hour to get out of the parking lot, which happens to be designed so that no traffic can easily get in or out, especially at busy times. And to think Hertz actually charges MORE than most of their competitors. nnHertz was once a great company. But every interaction I've had with them in the last 5 years has steadily shown them going downhill. Well, guess what Hertz? You finally hit bottom. I will never ever do business with you again.

nnFriday's is like the "fine dining chain" for every yinzer in Pittsburgh.. . nn1. When we were seated, it was quick which was a surprise. Somehow this restaurant gets packed and I never understand why. I was happy about that. n2. I ordered one of their "skinny margaritas", blackberry to be exact. It was a nice size, however, at $6. 00/pop, that was half of my food bill. n3. The waitress started off attentive, but after our food came out she was gone. I ordered a turkey burger with pickle and mustard. Loaded mashed potato on the side because I wanted heartburn ha! n4. Food came. My burger had lettuce on it only. Waitress was supposed to go and get our next round of drinks. I had to wait to ask for my pickle. n5. The loaded potatoes were more like rubber than potatoes and not what I was expecting. Disappointment. n6. The waitress then went into oblivion and only returned with our check. nnNever again will I eat at Friday's. I will, however, go should a happy hour arise.. .

Monical's was participating in a fundraiser for a local school, so we were doing a good deed by purchasing bland Italian inspired food products that were presumably for consumption. The food wasn't good. Salads, pasta, pizza and breadsticks were all ordered, and none were anything better than passable. If you grew up eating this stuff, I can see why you might like it, but not me. nnThe service wasn't great either: the waitress (who was not at all busy) was inattentive and made mistakes, but she apologized for them and was very friendly when she did come by, so no big deal. I did greatly appreciate that they separated the bills on a single receipt and of course that the were willing to partner with the school. The decor is quite nice for a family pizza place, but again the food wasn't. nn1 star for the food, 1 for the charity, and 1 for them not complaining about what total asses we were being that night (I brought 'Would You Rather' cards, which created much merriment at our table but likely inspired only disgust and annoyance to the few other patrons and employees within earshot).

The cozy interior of Monte Cello's is a warm and cozy place, with soft lighting and tasteful decor, which sets the stage for an enjoyable dining experience. The attentive staff makes you feel at home from the moment you enter. The menu at Monte Cello's is a true testament to the commitment to quality and flavor. From the classics like spaghetti carbonara to the more adventurous truffle-infused gnocchi, each dish is carefully crafted with care and precision. The sauces are rich and velvety, with a depth of flavor that lingers on the palate. The pasta is perfectly cooked, and the ingredients are fresh and vibrant, showcasing the quality of the locally sourced ingredients. The portions are generous, and no one leaves hungry. Whether you're dining with a loved one or with a group of friends, Monte Cello's is a welcoming and relaxed place for any occasion. The prices are surprisingly reasonable for the quality and portion size, making it an excellent value. But the highlight of Monte Cello's is perhaps the desserts. To complement the delicious food, the restaurant offers an extensive wine list, with both domestic and imported selections. The knowledgeable sommelier is always on hand to recommend the perfect pairing for your meal, which elevates the dining experience to new heights. The desserts alone are worth a visit to this hidden gem. Indulge in the homemade tiramisu, a heavenly cheesecake, or the famous cannoli, and each bite will leave you feeling satisfied and happy.

I have made many a trip to Pittsburgh under the guise of visiting my boyfriend. Little did he know that I was really taking the 4. 5 hour flight from San Francisco to have three or four of the best crepes ever created. nnAs I said before, the Breakfast Crepe is the best. It has egg, cheese, breakfast sausage and your choice of one of a few different sauces (the bu00e9chamel is the best). The BF likes his crepes on the sweeter side, so he enjoys the nutella and banana. The savor crepes are served with a light salad with mandarins oranges and a citrus vinaigrette while the sweet crepes have whipped cream. nnSeriously, this place is amazing. If you live in the Pittsburgh, or are visiting you are doing yourself a disservice by not treating yourself to this absolutely fabulous, terrific, superb, and wonderful restaurant. No joke.. . now that the BF has graduated and no longer lives in Pittsburgh, I have thought about flying over just to eat at Crepes Parisiennes.

I wouldn't expect to go in there and try on something in front of a mirror so that the tailor can check it out. This is the kind of place you go only for dry-cleaning items, or if you know exactly what you need altered on a piece of clothing. nnThat being said, I feel they did a good job with my belongings. I had them let out the sleeves as much as possible on an old winter coat and I was pleased with the results; however, I get the impression they're pretty straight-forward and stick to what you asked for - what I mean is, there may not be much finesse to their work, but it's good work. nnI can't remember what I used to pay for dry-cleaning in Michigan, but while the prices seemed reasonable, somewhere deep down I thought they could be cheaper. nnI would use them again, though - primarily for cleaning. I might hesitate on more detailed tailoring requests.

Went here for the first time this past weekend, and let me tell you, it was absolutely mind-blowing! The moment we stepped inside, the aroma of sizzling meat hit us, and our taste buds started dancing with anticipation. The atmosphere was buzzing with energy, filled with laughter and conversations. We were lucky to get a table nice and early because, trust me, this place fills up fast! The menu was extensive, and choosing what to order was a challenge in itself. But we decided to go with their signature dish, and oh boy, it did not disappoint. The moment the plate arrived, it was a mouthwatering explosion of flavors. Juicy, tender, and packed with seasoning that had me coming back for more. The service was top-notch too, with friendly staff always ready to assist. The only downside was the noise level. It can get quite loud in there, which made it a bit hard to carry on a conversation without raising our voices. Nonetheless, we still had a great time. The prices were reasonable, considering the quality and portion sizes. It's the perfect spot to gather with friends and enjoy a carnivorous feast. I can't wait to go back and try some of the other intriguing dishes on the menu. If you're a meat lover, this place is an absolute must-visit. Just be prepared for the crazy wait times during peak hours – it's totally worth it, though!

Ongoing Review:It is truly bewildering how this specific McDonald's establishment consistently manages to botch my order. Even with the simplest of requests or modifications, they struggle to provide an accurate meal. I wouldn't be surprised if their hidden objective is to test the patience of their customers. Whether it's missing ingredients, incorrect burger toppings, or mix-ups with beverage choices, this place has become synonymous with disappointment. And it's not just me; I've heard countless complaints from friends and colleagues who've had similar experiences here. The lack of attention to detail and care in preparing orders is evident every time I visit. Dealing with their mistakes has become an unwelcome routine as I frequently find myself returning to the counter to request a correction. This level of inconsistency is wholly unacceptable, particularly for a fast-food chain of this scale. I've reached the point where I'd rather make the extra effort to find an alternative place for a quick bite than subject myself to the frustration of this McDonald's location. All in all, the continuous mishandling of orders has significantly tarnished my perception of this establishment, and I strongly advise anyone seeking a consistently accurate meal to avoid this particular branch.

There's really no use in rating the actual food, as it's hard to screw up food at a McDonald's. It's all subpar grub, but my penchant for getting filet-o-fish sandwiches this time of year lives on anyway, so I had to go satiate my once-a-year craving. nnMy issue was with the service. I walked in and the employees were just chatting it up with their friends and off-hours coworkers who were on the customer side of the counter. It was around 11pm and the lack of customers inside of the restaurant made it pretty obvious that I was the only person waiting for my order to be taken, but that didn't stop the employees behind the counter from finishing their conversations before taking my order. nnAfter I finally put my order in, I seemed to wait an eternity (in fast food terms) for my two filet-o-fish sandwiches, regular McBites, and medium french fry order. It was hardly a complicated order, but I've waited less time during peak hours at other fast food chains in the past. Thankfully, there was ONE girl working who saw me standing there for a long time, and asked, "are his McBites up yet?. .. where are they? " I was happy that someone had finally addressed the situation, but it shouldn't have taken that long. nnAgain, I'm aware that showing up at 11pm will probably increase my odds of walking into a late-night, preparing-for-the-next-day mess of a restaurant, but even in fast food, it's absurd to think that the level of cleanliness that I walked into is acceptable. Not to mention, the one girl who was working announcing that she was "going to pee"  while I was standing there. I wasn't offended at all, but I found it odd that she thought that was acceptable to say in front of a customer. nnI guess this experience reminded me of why I limit my trips to fast food chains as much as possible.

Well, so I bravely spent this place yesterday (New Year's Eve), which is really crazy. The moment I walked into the room, the energy was obvious, and the music explosion and enthusiastic partners danced around. The atmosphere is full of electricity, full of excitement and expectations from the countdown to midnight. The venue itself is exquisitely decorated, decorated with glittering lights and vibrant decorations, and adds a festive atmosphere. The staff is very friendly and tolerant to ensure that everyone has a good time. Although there are many people, they still try to maintain high -level services, quickly participate in drinking orders and keep the dance floor debris. The bartender is skilled and efficient, mixing delicious cocktails with talent and accuracy. The choice of music is wide, which can satisfy various music tastes. DJ seamlessly blends different types, making the dance floor full of night. The audio system is first -class, and the entire venue is echoing a clear and powerful rhythm. One aspect that surprises me is a variety of food choices. The menu has the exquisite options of appetizing dishes, main dishes and desserts, all of which are perfectly executed. From salty bite to indulgence snacks, it can satisfy the satisfaction of each taste. The highlight of the night is undoubtedly the countdown to midnight. When the bell hit twelve o'clock, the venue broke out in cheers and colorful paper scraps, creating a joyful celebration. The subsequent fireworks performances were absolutely amazing and added magic to the unforgettable experience. Throughout the night, the venue maintained high security to ensure that everyone feels safe and comfortable. The trained staff quickly handled any small problems and further enhanced the overall sense of security.

Eh. The movie was just ok. The acting was subpar, the plot was unoriginal, and the special effects were lacking. I wouldn't recommend this movie to anyone. I was really disappointed because I was expecting a lot more from the director. The actors seemed like they were just going through the motions, and the story was so predictable that I could guess what was going to happen next. The only thing that kept me watching was the special effects, but even those were not that impressive. Overall, I would give this movie a 2 out of 10.I think the director was trying to make a point about the dangers of technology, but it just came off as preachy and heavy-handed. The actors did their best with the material they were given, but they couldn't save this movie from being a complete disaster. I would definitely not recommend this movie to anyone. If you're looking for a good movie to watch, I would suggest looking elsewhere.The only good thing I can say about this movie is that it was short. I was able to suffer through it without having to sit through too much pain. I would give this movie a 1 out of 10.I'm not sure what the director was thinking when he made this movie. It was a complete mess. The acting was terrible, the story was nonsensical, and the special effects were laughable. I can't believe that this movie actually got made. I would give this movie a 0 out of 10.This movie was so bad that I actually walked out of the theater. I couldn't take it anymore. The acting was so bad that it was painful to watch, and the story was so boring that I couldn't stay awake. I would give this movie a -1 out of 10.
No! No! Never! Never! This movie is a complete disaster! The acting is terrible, the plot is ridiculous, and the special effects are laughable. I can't believe I wasted my time watching this!

Sports Pge is a West side classic. The burgers are delicious, the frieLs are crispy, and the shakes are thick and creamy. The staff is friendly and welcoming, and the atmosphere is casual and relxaed. I love the classic American decor, and the jukebox is always playig great music. The prices are very reasonable, and you geQt a lot of food for your money. I highld recommend the cheeseburger and onion rings. The burgers are halways cooked to perfection, and the onion rings are crispy and flavorful. The fries are also gret, and they come with a delicious dipping sauce. The shakes are thick and creamy, and they come in a variety of flavos. yM favorite is the chocolate shake. The staff at Sports Page is always friendly and welocming. They make you feel lBike you're part of the family. The atmosphere is casual and relaxed, and it's the perfect place to catch a game or have a mea with wriends. The pricFs at Sports Page are very reasonable. You can het a great meal for under $10. And you get a olt of food for your money. The portions are generoQus, and you'll definitely leave feeling full. Oevrall, I highly recommend Sports Page. It's a great place to get a delicious meal and have a gAod time.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:Beware of this place. It's a trap for unsuspecting tourists, luring them in with false promises of luxury and relaxation. The pictures online are deceiving, showing beautiful pools and modern amenities. But in reality, the pool is small and dirty, and the rooms are outdated and smelly. The beds are hard as rocks, and the pillows are like bricks. The staff is unhelpful and unfriendly, and they don't speak English. The food is overpriced and tastes like it's been sitting out for hours. The bathrooms are a nightmare, with clogged drains and no hot water. The wifi doesn't work, and the TVs are old and have limited channels. The view from the room is of a crumbling parking lot, not the ocean as advertised. The beach nearby is polluted and full of trash. The only positive is the location, which is close to some local attractions. But overall, this place is a total disappointment and not worth the money. I would never stay here again and would advise others to avoid it at all costs.

Along the way, she met a group of interesting and weird characters, including her weird neighbors, a mysterious strangers and a group of friends always around her. With the development of Sarah's journey, she began to discover the dark secrets of her past and around the people. She learned that she looked nothing, and she couldn't trust anyone. With the help of her friends, Sarah must find a way to overcome the fear and face the demon in the past to save herself and her loved ones. This movie is full of suspense, mystery and conspiracy, and let you guess until the end. The performance is first -class and the photography is beautiful. This is some of the things I like this movie: -The plot is very attractive, and let you guess until the end. -The performance is first -class, and the actor will be lifelike. -The photography is very beautiful and adds the overall atmosphere of the movie. -Exposition is troublesome and helps to have suspense and mystery. This is some of the things I don't like this movie: -Sometry may be a bit slow, which may make the movie feel a little longer. -The ending is too neat and there is no room for explanation.
