Both times I've gone have been for bands, and both times were fairly different experiences. nnMy first time was a one-star experience: it was ridiculously hot and humid inside, the staff lacked any sort of motivation for good customer service, and they crammed the 7- or 8-piece band in the front of the bar near the window, making for awkward viewing and playing. The sound was terrible and I spent more time outside than in. nnMy second time was a better experience, but still not one that would cause me to suggest other people go there. This time around it was a reasonable temperature in the bar, but that may only be due to the fact that it was in the 50s outside. The bands played in the back of the bar in an alcove/side room deal and it made a lot more sense. The sound was still deafening and subject to random strangers twiddling the knobs on the mixer, but overall not too bad. nnFree music is the best thing (only thing? ) Mr. Roberts has going for it. Its biggest downsides: n- it's touted as a blue-collar bar, but it's more an excuse to be shabby and dirty than anything; $15 for a 5-bottle bucket of beer is not a good deal, and $5 for a PBR & a rail shot is a special for a couple hours one night a week when it should be the standard price for such a combinationn- the ladies room is ridiculously tiny and awkward, had a seat that was half hanging off the bowl, and no TPn- every surface was dirty or sticky, yucknnYou probably won't die if you go to see a friend's band play, but supporting a friend should be the only motivation for setting foot inside.

Rolled in at 10am to meet a handful of people. Because it is Labor Day weekend, the place was empty except for a woman who had just gotten there too which was nice because a friend had warned me that the place gets crazy. We sat outside and atmosphere was cozy under the tent with the warm colors of the decor. Our waitress was incredibly friendly and presented us with the menus. I think it is great they have a prix fixe brunch - 1 alchy or nonalchy drink, an appetizer and an entree - but for $24. It looks like they have since raised the prices since Miles O. went. I felt it was just a tad steep and a lot of food but took the plunge anyway. I ordered the smoked salmon for the appetizer and the mushroom omlette for my entree, and had a hot coffee (they did not have ice coffee though offered to put ice with hot coffee if I wanted it). The waitress brought out some delicious mini baked treats before we got our dishes - poppy scone (on the crispier side but delicious), blueberry muffins and biscuits, which were all buttery goodness. After eating these I was getting a little full and then after my appetizer I realized I probably should not have gotten the prix fixe but could have would have should have. I ended up boxing half of my omlette entree. Portions were large, atmosphere was cool, food was good and wait staff were awesome but in the end I don't think $24 (not including the tip and tax) was worth it.

I asked if they offered free internet. Simple question, right? She hesitated and then went on this whole diatribe about how it used to be free, and then it wasn't and now it kinda is, but she wasn't sure. I swear that's exactly what she said. So she hesitantly gives me this paper, pulled off from a whole stack mind you, of the Internet name and password. At this point I'm confused.. . do you not want me to be here? Is it free? I finally got my answer when I logged on with no issues, it's free. Why all the hassle?? nnThe only reason I can see (and seriously a big one) is the coffee shop hanger on-ers. I can only imagine how this has become an epidemic in spots like this. Hip spots with lots of students and recent grads who want to scope out the free internet havens. I get it.. .. the coffee shop wants you to get your coffee, hang out for approximately 2 hours and leave. I'm sure this is not the case.. . and that people hang out enough that they could easily put on an apron and handle orders behind the counter. So long story short, this place is PACKED. Packed like you usually have to take a couple laps to find a free table.. .. or even lone chair! It's a little annoying. Plus, you're most likely going to see what the person next to you/on top of you is doing. Oh, sweet game of chess there dude.. .. nnOh yeah, my chai was okay, Oregon Chai Mix (my third fav after a homemade mix or Intellegentsia). Made exactly how I ordered it, extra hot, which a lot of people do not do correctly (it needs to burn my tongue, for realz). nnBut for me, this is a spot I'd avoid.. .. way too crowded!

Well, the steaks are quality, and my filet was done Pittsburgh rare just as I asked. All the sides are pretty pedestrian steak-house fare. I had heard numerous rave reviews about Enrico's, so my expectations were high as I entered the restaurant. The ambiance immediately caught my attention, with dimmed lighting and soft jazz music creating an intimate and inviting atmosphere. The attentive staff promptly showed us to our table, and the menu presented an array of enticing options.To start off, we ordered the bruschetta appetizer, and it exceeded all expectations. The toasted ciabatta bread was perfectly crunchy on the outside and delightfully soft on the inside, complemented by a generous topping of ripe tomatoes, fresh basil, and drizzles of balsamic glaze. It was a sensational blend of flavors that had us eagerly anticipating the rest of our meal.For the main course, I opted for their signature dish, the osso buco. The slow-braised veal shank was impeccably tender and fell off the bone with the slightest touch of my fork. It was served with a rich, aromatic sauce that was infused with herbs and accompanied by a velvety risotto. Each bite was a symphony of succulent meat and comforting flavors that reaffirmed why this dish was a favorite among patrons.My friend chose the seafood linguine, which boasted an exquisite medley of shrimp, scallops, and calamari tossed in a flavorful tomato and white wine sauce. The seafood was impeccably cooked, tender, and bursting with freshness. The pasta itself was perfectly al dente, and the sauce coated it evenly, leaving a lingering taste of the sea with every mouthful.As we were avid dessert enthusiasts, we couldn't resist indulging in a sweet finish. The tiramisu at Enrico's was simply divine. It had all the elements you would expect—a delicate balance of mascarpone cream, soaked ladyfingers, and a hint of rich cocoa powder. Each bite was a piece of heaven, and we savored it until the last spoonful.Throughout the dining experience, the service at Enrico's was exceptional.

We rarely eat at hibachi grills, but decided it sounded good tonight. The food was okay. Life's too short and there are way too many good things to eat to settle for okay! nnI think one thing that makes it tricky when reviewing a hibachi experience is that they all basically have the same menu - right down to the salad dressing! Therefore, comparisons are natural. nnI found the soup salty. The salad dressing was okay, but missing something. Rice was good, noodles had a nice garlic flavor and veggies were standard. I ordered the teriyaki steak and it had zero teriyaki flavor. Had the sauce not been visible I would have sworn I got the wrong item. The meat itself did not taste good. It wasn't spoiled tasting, more like a really cheap cut of meat. I only ate about three bites. In another environment I would have gone so far as to complain, but not here. The shrimp were good. Maybe most disappointing was the ubitiqous white shrimp sauce. It was very watery and bland: (. Major disappointment as that stuff is the highlight of a hibachi visit! In sum, the food was filling, but not good enough to come here again. nnThe positives are that the staff were friendly and worked quickly. The chef gave extra attention to my young son which was kind of him. Portions were quite large. I cannot imagine complaints about portion size!

I was really looking forward to trying out this restaurant, but unfortunately, it did not live up to my expectations. The service was slow and inattentive, and the food was just average. I ordered the buffet, which had a good selection of dishes, but the quality was not great. The eggs benedict was overcooked and the crab legs were too salty. The oysters were also watery and lacked flavor. The grilled cheese sandwich my friend ordered was also a disappointment. The bread was average and the cheese was not melted properly. The tomato soup that came with it was the highlight of the meal, but it was not enough to save the dish.The server was friendly, but it took a long time to get our food and refill our drinks. We had to ask the floor manager for the check because our server was nowhere to be found. The busing of the used plates was also slow, and there was a period of time when no one was manning the carving station. Overall, I would not recommend this restaurant to anyone. It was not worth the money and the service was subpar.

nnMonty's Blue Plate Diner is located in a quiet but neat part of East Madison, not far off Willy Street. It looks like an old gas station (could be), and there was a 25 minute wait for Saturday brunch when we came. There's space to sit outside, or shops nearby to wander to. Inside it's classic diner, through and through. We (4 of us) were seated at a small booth. It was cozy to say the least. nnKatie K. and I split a Chocolate Shake ($6) to start off. It's what you'd expect from the setting, served in a metal tin and absolutely delicious. I as good as chugged mine. nnI leaned toward lunch this time around and got the Roast Chicken, Bacon & Goat Cheese sandwich ($9). This dish was just missing a wow-factor (in addition to the Oxford-comma). I love goat cheese, and expected so much with it melted between two pieces of sourdough and chicken, bacon, and tomato. But none of the flavors that should've been present stood out. The salty bacon was lost in the grease of the bread. There may not have been any tomato. The chicken was bland. nnI loved the shake, and the sandwich was by no means bad, but there was no warm-feeling deliciousness I expected from a meal in such a diner. I'd return, but get something else.

We made reservations as they had a promotion for National Cheeseburger Day and there were six of us. When my friend and I got there, we told the hostess that we were meeting friends and had a reservation under another friend's name, and she shrugged at us three times before asking us how many were in our party and never bothered to find our reservation. We told her we had an infant with us, and she looked annoyed to have to accommodate a baby. She eventually asked us if we wanted to wait for the others in our party or sit down, and then walked us to our table and told us nothing about who our server was or ever cracked a smile. nnWe sat for at least 10 minutes with no service and eventually flagged down a waiter, who found our server, who arrived about 5 minutes later. The drink service was incredibly slow, and the food service was okay. Our burgers were average, which was disappointing since we've come there often and enjoyed the burgers. It seems their buns and meat have changed and the quality is not as good as it had been. nnA disappointing trip, and we likely won't be back anytime soon.

Take me out to the ballgame! The crack of the bat, the roar of the crowd, the smell of the fresh-cut grass - there's nothing quite like it. I love going to baseball games, and I'm always up for a good time at the ballpark.One of my favorite things about baseball games is the atmosphere. There's something about being surrounded by thousands of other fans, all cheering for the same team, that's really special. It's also great to see the players up close and personal. You can really appreciate their athleticism and skill when you're watching them play in person.Of course, no baseball game would be complete without some good food. I always get a hot dog and a beer when I go to a game. And if the team is winning, I might even splurge on a souvenir.I've been to a lot of baseball games over the years, and I've seen some great games. I've seen no-hitters, walk-off home runs, and even a few World Series games. But no matter what the game, I always have a good time at the ballpark.Baseball is America's pastime, and it's easy to see why. It's a great game that can be enjoyed by people of all ages. If you've never been to a baseball game, I highly recommend it. You won't be disappointed.

The food is very nicely prepared & laid out before you with a fair amount of simplicity, no really outrageous creative plating going on. The menu seemed fairly traditional, I don't recall seeing the more "Americanized" (what I consider) "crazy" rolls that many trendy sushi restaurants (like NYC's & PHL's Haru) offer. And don't take that as a bad thing. While I'll happily admit to liking those "crazy Americanized" rolls, its nice to find a great more traditional place out there. From what I understand Mr Shu is very stringent in regards to his sushi standards, to the point that he does not allow his sous chefs to prepare sushi if he is not around to supervise. nnThe restaurant itself is small & cozy (& up a loooooooong flights of stairs) & without the loud banging tunes you find in the more trendy restaurants so that a great conversation is possible to have whether you're there on a date or catching up with a friend. nnI wanted to try the omakase, but opted against it as Umi was my second dinner of the evening (I'm a pig, I know), however I have heard pretty mixed reviews on whether or not its worth it. nnThe service was simply OK.  Kind of slow. No, more like definitely slow. Nothing special. Still gave the waitress a 20%+ tip though so obviously I still wouldn't call the service horrendous.

So walking around the cultural district after the parade I had to come here. nnWe got there for lunch around noon. If you expect to be treated like a king, don't come here. It's a pretty standard Jiffy Lube, with the same level of service you'd expect from any other location. The staff is friendly and helpful, but they're not going to roll out the red carpet for you. The prices are reasonable, and the work is done quickly and efficiently. But if you're looking for a luxurious experience, you're better off going to a different shop.I've been to this Jiffy Lube several times for oil changes and other basic maintenance, and I've always been satisfied with the service. The staff is always courteous and professional, and they've never tried to upsell me on unnecessary services. The prices are comparable to other Jiffy Lube locations, and the work is always done quickly and efficiently.However, this Jiffy Lube is not the Ritz-Carlton. The waiting area is small and cramped, and there's no free coffee or Wi-Fi. If you're looking for a comfortable and luxurious experience, you're better off going to a different shop.But if you're just looking for a quick and affordable oil change, Jiffy Lube is a good option. The staff is friendly and helpful, the prices are reasonable, and the work is done quickly and efficiently.One thing to keep in mind is that this Jiffy Lube is often very busy. If you're planning on going on a weekend, be prepared to wait. The line can sometimes be out the door.Overall, I'm satisfied with the service at this Jiffy Lube. It's not the most luxurious experience, but it's a good option for a quick and affordable oil change.

How on earth did this place win any award for great food and service is beyond me. nnnThe menu was extremely old, small and the coffee was pure water. nI order the walleye, hash browns, toast and eggs breakfast. nMy mother order biscuits and gravynnMy toast was cold, hash browns had no tastes, over easy eggs were not cooked fully and fish was very small in size. nnMothers gravy was too salty and a thick bunch of sop. nnThe menu had no coordination. We did not receive such treatment. She passed our table several times and never once asked us how was the food or why our orders were taking so long. Not once. The food is always good, the service is always friendly, and the atmosphere is always warm and inviting. However, my most recent experience at Cracker Barrel was not so positive. The country fried chicken, which is usually my favorite dish at Cracker Barrel, was dry and overcooked. The mashed potatoes were also lumpy, and the green beans were bland. nnAs a vegetarian, having so many options in one restaurant can be quite novel. Their menu is extensive, for both meat and vegetarian dishes. Oh, and the soups! There's veggie wonton, egg drop, corn soup, and vegetarian hot and sour! JOY! The entrees list is far too long to get into, but I'll say that proteins include tofu and soy protein "meat". My personal favorite and go-to entree is kung pao soy (it is really good with extra spice if you like that sort of thing) and veggie wonton and egg drop soups are my favorite. A nice pot of tea accompanies your visit and the entree potions are large enough to share if you are in the sharing mood. They also have a large lunch menu that includes a big portion of the dish you order and a soup. nnThe atmosphere at China Palace is pretty relaxed and calming, with minty green walls and dark cherry-stained chairs. The dining room is spacious and can easily accommodate groups of 10-12. nnVegetarians, if you are feeling unsure about what is in your dish, look for the manager (owner maybe? ) who seems to be a constant fixture there and who has a complete knowledge of the menu items. He'll gladly quell your fears and help you out with trustworthy information.

I cannot believe this place has not been reviewed yet. I guess it is a best kept secret. nnThere were four in our party and we ordered a variety of sandwiches that ranged from a traditional Reuben, turkey and Swiss on a bagel to a pastrami and Swiss on marble rye and a turkey and cheese on a bread type that escapes me. Regardless, all the sandwiches were very good to excellent. The meat was lean and adequately piled on. The vegatables on the sandwiches were very fresh. All sandwiches came with a Kosher pickle spear and we opted for bags of Snyder of Hanover chips. I haven't had Snyder chips since I left Pittsburgh eons ago. nnThe amount of sandwiches offered were many. The staff were efficient and three-rivers friendly. nnI failed to realize that they offered Matzo ball soup. I will have to save that for a return trip to PGH. Since the in-laws live here, that's definitely going to happen.

The lobby is small and cramped, with a tiny check-in desk that always seems to have a long line. The elevators are slow and often break down, leaving guests to trudge up and down the stairs. The rooms are tiny and poorly maintained, with stained carpets and scratched up furniture. The beds are uncomfortable and the pillows are flat and lumpy. The bathrooms are even worse, with leaky faucets and clogged drains that often leave the floor flooded. The hotel's WiFi is spotty and unreliable, and the TVs are old and have limited channels. The hotel's restaurant is overpriced and serves mediocre food, and the bar is always understaffed and takes forever to get a drink. The gym is tiny and poorly equipped, and the pool is small and often closed for maintenance. The hotel's location is convenient, but the surrounding area is sketchy and filled with homeless people. The staff is friendly and tries to be helpful, but they seem to be overwhelmed by the hotel's many problems. Overall, this hotel is a disappointment and not worth the money.

We had my niece and nephew last weekend (ages 6 and 3) so we decided to take them there to check out the birds. nnI enjoyed the center and felt the price was right for the amount of exhibits and time it takes to get around ($10 adults, free kids under 4). The Raptor Center is a sanctuary for birds who would not make it in the wild, and they go to schools to educate young kids about raptors, so I didn't exactly feel bad paying since I knew it was going to a good cause. They had several different kinds of owls, eagles, hawks, vultures and other predatory birds. Most of the habitats seemed to be a decent size for the animal inside, and the birds themselves seemed relatively comfortable. There was also a little playground based on Harry Potter for the kids to play in, and they liked that. The staff there were very friendly and informative, and several who were transporting a bird from one area to the next were more than happy to take a few seconds to tell us a little about the bird and let the kids get a good look at it. nnMy nephew really enjoyed looking at all the birds, and he even knew a little on his own since the center had brought some birds over to his school. My three year old niece didn't care for it as much and was more interested picking rocks and thistles out of the dirt then looking at birds in cages. I was really glad that she was free and we didn't have to pay for her! nnOverall, it's a really clean, friendly, and educational experience. Check out some cool looking birds while walking the trails and learn about these beautiful raptors. Great for kids and adults.

No. Her sofa from Gene Sane arrived. It made her just as happy. nnShe and I went to Gene Sane a few months ago and found fabric for a sofa and chair. The fabric was not inexpensive. Some of that they have is really beautiful designer fabric, some of it is not so special and I am convinced could be found elsewhere for less. However, they did have excellent customer service throughout the entire upholstering process. And my parents ended up getting two sofas and three chairs done there. nnMy parents live in Oakland in a condo building with a ridiculously small elevator. Gene Sanes went above and beyond to make sure this extremely long sofa from my grandma would fit in the elevator at my parents' building. They brought the sofa mid-upholstery to the building just to make sure it would fit into the elevator. That is such great customer service it is ridiculous. It did fit, which is great, but their backup plan was to upholster it as two pieces which would then seamlessly be fit together as one inside the apartment. This is the kind of stuff that high end places in NYC know how to do and even though they ended up not having to do it in this case, it was good to know they have the skills to do so. They were also great with attention to detail of lining up all the stripes on all the cushions both front and back. These are the kinds of things that make a big difference.

5 star, if that were allowed.. . nnAn unpretentious little neighborhood spot, this bar and grill has a 'sports bar' theme but a very relaxed, although almost too clean for,  near-dive-y feel. Not crammed with student crowds, and a good mix of ages although many boomer types were represented. nnOn U of I basketball nights, they serve free chili. Our dinner plans canceled abruptly, two housemates and I headed over to check it out. nnExpecting a watery, bean heavy chili soup; I was pleasantly shocked by the meaty and rich actuality. nnIt was sooo good that we all decided to stop by sometime to try menu items we'd actually pay for in cash! nnDrinks were good, and strong after they realized we'd tip. The bar was crowded, but not unpleasantly. Only complaint was that the fresh diced onion and grated cheese toppers were not refilled after they ran out.

I was also nervous because they wanted to charge me $ 75 just to come out and give me an estimate! I was hesitant to pay that, but the company told me they would reimburse me if I used the company they chose. So I sucked it up and paid, hoping for the best. The technician who came out was very friendly and professional. He explained everything that needed to be done and gave me a quote for the work. The price was reasonable, so I agreed to have the work done. The technician got right to work and finished in a few hours. He did a great job and I was very happy with the results. I would definitely recommend this company to others. I'm glad I went ahead and used them despite the negative reviews. They were professional, efficient, and did a great job.

This branch was recently expanded, which is a good thing, because it is always absolutely slammed (perhaps a change over when Joy wrote her review).This location is small and gets very busy. ). Kind of dresses itself up to be a nice sit-down place, but unfortunately, the food was not on the same level. nnFOOD: nFor appetizers, I shared the Calamari with my friend and for the main entree, I got the recommended Pasta Trio. nnFirst off, I love calamari. The recommended "Pasta Trio" (3 types of pastas and "all you can eat") was a bit below average. For a recommendation, I expected more. This was the first time that she did not finish her dish (Chicken Cacciatore). OG just tastes better and is way cheaper. nnSERVICE: nMediocre at best. The waters were not kept filled. Not a huge deal for me since I drink slowly, but some of my friends kept looking for the water and it clearly distracted them from their dining experience. The Pasta Trio is "all you can eat" and they come back to refill your plate. A few of us were waiting on the third round.. . and we never got it. Even though the food was not the tastiest, we were hungry and still needed to be fed. The wait staff didn't even ask if we wanted a third serving, and just took our plates quickly without giving us a chance. A few of us were still left a little bit hungry.. . it was a grumpy ride home: ( nnIMPRESSIONS: nRelative to the pricing of the food, I expected better quality. Let me start by saying that many of the reviews here are unfair. This is a great game that deserves more credit than it gets. The graphics are beautiful, the gameplay is fun and engaging, and the story is interesting and well-written. I highly recommend this game to anyone who enjoys action RPGs.One of the things I love most about this game is the combat. It's fast-paced and exciting, and there's a lot of variety in the enemies you'll face. You'll need to use all of your skills to defeat your opponents, and it's always satisfying to take down a tough boss.The story is also very well done. It's full of interesting characters and twists and turns, and I was always eager to see what would happen next. The voice acting is also top-notch, and it really helps to bring the characters to life.The graphics are also very impressive. The environments are detailed and immersive, and the character models are well-animated. The game also runs very smoothly, even on older hardware.I highly recommend this game to anyone who enjoys action RPGs. It's a great game that deserves more credit than it gets.However, the game isn't without its flaws. The load times can be a bit long, and the inventory system could be improved. But overall, this is a great game that I highly recommend.

nnBit of a complex review. My biggest gripe with Casbah is that it's Casbah Mediterranean when the menu (at least on this night, but I don't think it changes all that much) offers very authentic Mediterranean dishes. One lamb dish (not overly Mediterranean) and no couscous or tagine, which are major staples of Mediterranean cuisine. Even a side of hummus and pita or veggies would be nice. Nonetheless, the menu has lots of fine options, just none that I've seen in a typical Mediterranean joint. I think it's a little misleading especially if you're seeking Mediterranean cuisine. nnWe sampled several of the cocktails, all of which were creative and well priced. They offer a wide variety of dishes to suit every taste and appetite, and the quality of the food and service is consistently high. The service was also excellent, with friendly, attentive servers who made sure our glasses were always full and our plates were never empty. Don't miss out on this hidden gem!"

The sport originated in Scotland in the 16th century and has only grown in popularity over the years. In fact, curling was even a demonstration sport at the 1998 Winter Olympic Games in Nagano, Japan. One of the reasons curling has become so popular is its accessibility. Unlike other winter sports, curling doesn’t require a high level of athleticism or specialized equipment. All you need is a broom, a curling stone, and a sheet of ice. Curling is a team sport, and each team consists of four players. This creates a sense of camaraderie and friendship among the team members, and many curlers have formed lasting bonds with their teammates. In recent years, curling has become even more accessible with the construction of new curling rinks and the development of portable curling rinks. In addition to its social benefits, curling also provides a great workout. It requires a combination of strength, endurance, and balance, and it’s a great way to stay active and healthy. The game is played by sliding stones down a sheet of ice, trying to get them as close to the target (also known as the “button”) as possible. This has allowed more people to try the sport, which has helped to increase its popularity. One of the most exciting aspects of curling is the strategy involved. The game requires a great deal of skill and strategy, as players must take into account the ice conditions, the wind, and the position of the other stones. Another appealing aspect of curling is its slow and methodical pace. It’s a great sport for people who enjoy strategy and precise movements.

at all. nnnI went to this location after seeing all of the reviews on yelp, which were mostly positive but I saw a few things wrong. nnThe hosted greeted me and my date immediately, and got us seated quickly since it was a Monday night and virtually empty except for the hibachi area, which was what I came for. nnSeating is wierd here, they had half empty hibachi tables but insisted on putting me and my date at an empty table. I had the worst experience at this restaurant. It was a Saturday night, and my friends and I decided to give it a try based on the positive reviews we had seen online. However, from the moment we walked in, I knew it was going to be a disappointing evening. The place was overcrowded, and there seemed to be a lack of organization. We were told there would be a 15-minute wait, but it turned into almost an hour before we finally got a table. Once we were seated, we hoped that the food would make up for the long wait. Unfortunately, that wasn't the case. I ordered their signature pasta dish, and it arrived lukewarm, as if it had been sitting under a heat lamp for too long. The pasta itself was overcooked and mushy, and the sauce lacked any depth of flavor. It tasted like something you could easily make at home with a jar of store-bought sauce. My friends' meals weren't any better. One of them ordered a steak that was supposed to be medium-rare but arrived well-done and tough. Another friend had a seafood dish, and the shrimp in it were overcooked and rubbery. It was clear that the kitchen was not paying attention to the quality of their ingredients or the cooking process.To make matters worse, the service was abysmal. Our server was inattentive and seemed overwhelmed by the number of tables she had to attend to. It took forever to get our drinks refilled, and when we tried to get her attention for any issues with our food, she would disappear into thin air. It was incredibly frustrating and made the whole dining experience even more unpleasant.As we looked around, we noticed that other diners were also expressing their dissatisfaction. Some were complaining about the long wait times, while others were sending their dishes back to the kitchen. It was clear that we were not the only ones having a terrible experience.In the end, we couldn't wait to get out of there. We asked for the check, and even that took an eternity to arrive.

nnThe food was good, as we have expected from a previous visit. nnThe service was OK. The restaurant was maybe half full on a Monday night. nnWe were passed from one server to another, even though the first server was still working. nnIn addition, the explanation of the 3-course special was incomplete, so it was not clear what was included or what choices were possible, but we did get all that should have been included. We also had a hard time getting attention for basic service items, like more cheese for the one person who ordered the pasta tasting trio, or the second glass of wine for the special. nnIn the end, we could not finish it all and asked for it to be boxed up. Three of us got our remainders boxes, but they apparently didn't box up the half of the secondi from my wife, who was really looking forward to finishing it the next night. We didn't notice until the next morning that they left out one of the take home boxes. nnMaybe the staff was just having an off night, but it will negatively effect my decision for a repeat visit. nnSummary: good, but the service was a bit disappointing for this level of restaurant.

From the moment I walked through the doors, I knew I was in for a truly horrendous time. The place was disorganized and chaotic, with no one seeming to have any idea what they were doing. The receptionist barely acknowledged me and seemed more interested in chatting with her coworkers than attending to the customers. As I waited for my appointment, I couldn't help but notice the uncleanliness of the waiting area. There were papers scattered everywhere, and the furniture looked like it hadn't been cleaned in ages.When my turn finally came, I was greeted by a hairstylist who seemed utterly disinterested in her job. She barely said a word to me and didn't bother asking about my preferences or desired style. It was clear that she just wanted to get through the appointment as quickly as possible. As she started cutting my hair, I immediately noticed that something wasn't right. The scissors felt dull, and she was handling them with little precision. It was as if she was just going through the motions without any care or skill.As the haircut progressed, I could see in the mirror that it was turning into a disaster. The stylist completely ignored my instructions and gave me a hairstyle that was nothing like what I wanted. The layers were uneven, and the overall shape was completely off. I tried to speak up and express my dissatisfaction, but she brushed me off and continued with her careless work. It was clear that she had no intention of rectifying her mistakes or even listening to my concerns.By the time she finished, I was horrified by the result. I looked like a complete mess, and it was evident that the stylist had no idea what she was doing. Other customers in the salon were staring at me, and I could hear whispers and muffled laughter. The embarrassment was unbearable, and I just wanted to get out of there as quickly as possible.I paid for the disastrous haircut, but I couldn't bring myself to leave a tip.

I'll briefly break down how I gauge them (I've been many times, both before and after the renovation)nnFacility:  Great environment, a reasonable number of lanes (although not as many as other alleys in the area), a roomy bar with TVs for the sports games, and an upstairs that has both pool as well as a rental area that hosts private functions or tournaments. nnCost:  Fairly cheap if you go on open bowling nights, I think it's around $1 or $2 a game plus shoes. I'm sure it's hiked up otherwise. Open Bowling is usually after 9pm during the week and not a bad option. We used to go on Thursdays which seems to be a good night for them. nnFood:  It's typical bar food. They have good cheese curds, and the usual fare that you'd see at an alley. Nothing to write home about but if you're in the mood for a greasy burger you probably won't go wrong. nnService:  I cannot stand the people that work at the counter. There's a couple of older guys (50-60+) who obviously hate life and want to project that on you when you're there. Everytime I've gone in I've tried to be courteous and polite, and asked simple basic questions like "I'd like to get a lane, there's 3 of us and we all need shoes" etc, etc. These guys first look at you like you walked into the wrong bar, and then respond with gruff, 2 second answers that doesn't help either party move the process along. It's really irritating because I've gone to this alley off and on for probably about 10 years or so, and it's always been like that. The bartenders, when you get a woman, are usually friendly and don't share the same attitude. nnPersonally I'm more of a Schwoeglers guy, but their prices for open bowling are more expensive and the atmosphere is noiser, so Sport Bowl sometimes wins out.

But, alas, time has not been kind to this establishment. The decor, while still charming in its own way, is starting to show its age. The once-pretty Victorian building now looks a bit worn and faded, with peeling paint and creaky floorboards. The stain-glass windows, while still beautiful, are now somewhat dimmed by the grime that has built up over the years. The grand piano in the bar area, once a lovely centerpiece, now sits silent and unused, propping up dusty bottles of wine. The pastel colors of the dining room, once soft and inviting, now seem dingy and faded. The 80's posters, while still amusing, are starting to look like relics of a bygone era. Overall, the restaurant's attempt to maintain a vintage charm has resulted in a space that feels more tired and worn out than retro-cool. Despite this, the food remains passable, and the service is still relatively friendly and attentive. However, one can't help but feel that this place has seen better days.

Since we weren't sure what to get and were just generically hungry, we decided to just let the menu decide for us and we ordered one of each of the highlighted items on the menu - the "house specials" according to our server. nnThe calamari was excellent and it was apparent that it had been hand breaded. The Asian fusion made for a delicious alternative to the typical calamari that was reminiscent of a General Tso's squid. The main dishes were also good, but nothing in particular stuck out about them. I also had a red-velved martini which I thought was probably the best part of the meal. nnOverall, I would definitely go back on a weeknight for happy hour or on a Tuesday to try the variety of mini martinis, but I think I'll leave paying $9 for an alcoholic beverage to "special" nights and not just a typical weekend dinner. nnThey do lose a star because even though we were one of three occupied tables and a few taken seats at the bar, the service was incredibly slow. It took so long for our appetizer to come out, I began to question whether the cook had to get in his car and go down to Wholey's to buy more squid.

We were greeted by friendly staff and walked through the room that smelled of wonderful steak. We were seated and our waiter was very friendly and knowledgeable. Everything was fine until we got the food which was disappointing. I ordered off the special menu and had the corn chowder, a filet and lobster tail, a side of creamed spinach and dessert for about $55. My husband had the cowboy ribeye for $65, the scallops to start and a side of brussel sprouts. nnThe corn chowder was ok but not very hot. The scallops which came recommended were ok but they were on a pesto sauce which was simply too overpowering for the delicate scallop. We used the pesto to dip the bread in. The steaks came and were both overcooked. They were supposed to be medium rare but my filet was well done and his was more like medium. We should have complained but we decided to just eat bc we didn't want to wait and didn't think that they would be less well done since they come on a scorching hot plate. My husband liked the brussel sprouts but my creamed spinach was not hot and was stringy. nnAgain, the food was mediocre but the staff was extremely friendly. I booked on opentable and told them that it was his birthday and they did bring him a complimentary dessert. I prefer Morton's, Ditkas and Peter Luger's.

The delivery date was "pushed back" many times until it finally came in over 3 months after they said it would, and gave me the run-around throughout the entire process. It wasn't packed at all but the hostess decided to sit us outside without asking where we'd prefer to sit. It was really hot that day so it was pretty uncomfortable. nnI immediately noticed that a lot of CMU undergrads worked there. While I was dining there I saw 3 CMU classmates. nnI started off with the gazpacho and it was very tasty. We were also given some bread with a lavender goat cheese spread. The spread was ok. nnFor the entrees I got the roasted pepper cavereccia that had crab in it and ricotta cavatelli with sausage. nnFor the cavereccia I thought there was going to be big chunks of crab but I was disappointed to see that the crab was shredded into small pieces. The pasta was a little undercooked and the sauce was ok. nnThe cavatelli was extremely oily. The more you ate it the more you saw the pool of oil it was swimming in. I had to wipe some of the oil off the pasta. nnLastly I had the dark chocolate raspberry bread pudding. It was pretty good but I wish there were more raspberries in it. nnService was pretty good and the decor outside was nice, although, it got really stuffy in the outdoor tent. nnThis place is pretty expensive and based on the food I wouldn't go back for that price.

I'm blown away by the sheer brilliance of this film. The script was a masterpiece, seamlessly blending intense drama, heartwarming humor and thought-provoking themes. It handled issues of love, loss and the human condition with such elegance that I was still thinking about it long after the credits rolled. The pacing was perfect, with a perfect balance between high-stakes action and quieter, more introspective moments. The music was a work of art, enhancing the emotions and enhancing every scene. There was never a dull moment. The sound design was also impeccable, with crisp, clear effects that added depth and realism to the world created on screen. It is disappointing that such a basic safety measure has been overlooked. It is commendable that the walls are regularly reset, but it does not compensate for the uncomfortable landing surfaces. Several times I landed with a jolt, which caused unnecessary strain on my joints. Moreover, the lack of responsiveness of the spring board makes it difficult to generate the momentum necessary for a successful climb. I often find myself struggling to gain the initial height needed to tackle the more difficult routes. This limitation can be frustrating, especially for climbers who are trying to improve their skills and push their limits. In addition to the safety concerns, the general upkeep of the gym leaves much to be desired. The lack of cleanliness is evident, with chalk dust and debris accumulating in the corners and crevices. It is disappointing to see a place that should be concerned with hygiene and presentation fall short in this regard. Another drawback is the limited variety of climbing routes available. It seems that after a few visits I have already exhausted most of the available options. The absence of an instructor or guide is also a missed opportunity. It would be beneficial to the appeal of the gym to introduce new routes and cater to climbers of different skill levels. The lack of adequate ventilation is another notable issue. Many climbers, especially beginners, would benefit from professional guidance and advice. Having someone experienced to offer tips, correct technique and offer support would enhance the overall climbing experience and create a sense of community within the gym. During busy periods the gym can become stuffy and humid.

I walked in with long hair and normal-looking bangs that all needed significant trimming. The stylist said he would need to take a couple of inches off of the length due to damage. I said that was fine. I did not ask for any other style changes. I somehow walked out with bangs that take up most of the front part of my head, much like a small child's haircut (to serve the purpose of keeping hair out of the child's face). He also took almost five inches off the length, instead of the "couple" promised. I feel like my haircut looks like a cross between a bowl cut, a female mullet, and the futuristic look of Mia Wallace from Pulp Fiction. I'm pretty open minded to different haircuts, but this one is truly awful, and it's too short now for me to even pull it back easily to try to make it look better. nnI'm going to a place in Shadyside today to see if they can maybe do extensions to remedy the mullet look, or implement some kind of other cut to make it look a little better. It's a shame, because this place is downtown and so convenient to my workplace, but I certainly will not risk it again. nn: (

nPlus if you make some wise menu choices, you can eat good without ingesting a whole lot of fat grams. This unique theater adds so much to the mix! It's a fantastic theater because they focus on plays, usually with one musical in the mix. There's new works here, like the original show The Chief, about what else, but Pittsburgh Steelers founding owner Art Rooney. They bring in the classics like A Midsummer Night's Dream and The Important of Being Earnest. They also have newer works right off Broadway, like The Rabbit Hole and Superior Donuts. Plus, they bring in Second City every year, which is big in my book because they've spawned a couple SNL cast members!! nnI absolutely LOVE the theater! I'm a big fan of a theater in the round. That means there's seating on 3 sides of the stage. They've also been able to do some really cool things with the stage/set, like making an actual pool for the show Metamorphoses. My favorite all time show was The Secret Letters of Jackie and Marilyn, which was a world premiere. The show was about a made up relationship between the two women where they wrote letters to each other. It was a VERY cool concept! nnThey also do a lot of great events like the Mix at 6 for young professionals before a show. Plus, they understand that most of us under 26 (and of course sometimes above) are broke ho's, they offer $15 tickets! The great thing is, there's really not a bad seat in the house! nnAgain, we have a fantastic theater district, so TAKE ADVANTAGE and go here! Don't make me tell you twice! !

The ice cream here is undeniably tasty, boasting a variety of flavors sure to please any palate. From classic options like vanilla and chocolate to inventive creations like salted caramel and cookie dough, there's something for everyone. The cones are expertly crafted, providing a satisfying crunch with each bite. However, while the ice cream excels in flavor, it falls slightly short of extraordinary due to its texture. It lacks the velvety smoothness and creaminess that would elevate it to greatness. Nevertheless, the sundaes are a delightful indulgence, generously topped with whipped cream, hot fudge, and a cherry on top. The splits are equally satisfying, with their generous portions of ice cream, fresh fruit, and decadent sauces creating a harmonious medley of flavors. As for the milkshakes, they are thick, rich, and wonderfully blended, transporting you to milkshake heaven with each sip. The ice cream soda floats are a nostalgic treat, offering the perfect balance of fizzy soda and creamy ice cream. Overall, while the ice cream might not be extraordinary, it still offers a delightful and enjoyable experience for any ice cream lover.

I would give this movie a rating of 3 out of 5 stars. While it had its moments, there were several aspects in which it fell short. Initially, the plot seemed promising, but the execution left much to be desired. The pacing was inconsistent, with some scenes dragging on and others feeling rushed and underdeveloped.Furthermore, the character development was lacking. The protagonist felt one-dimensional, making it difficult to empathize with their journey. The supporting cast had potential, but they were severely underutilized, resulting in missed opportunities to explore their stories and motivations.From a technical standpoint, the cinematography was decent, showcasing breathtaking visuals in certain sequences. However, the editing was choppy, making it hard to follow the narrative at times. Additionally, the sound mixing was imbalanced, with the dialogue often getting drowned out by the overpowering background score.The film's attempt at incorporating humor fell flat as well. The jokes felt forced and failed to elicit genuine laughter. It seemed like the comedic relief was added hastily, without much thought to its integration within the overall tone of the film.On the positive side, the production design was impressive, effectively capturing the essence of the story's setting. The costumes and set pieces were meticulously crafted, enhancing the visual appeal of the movie. The soundtrack, although overshadowed by the flawed sound mixing, had a few standout tracks that added depth to certain scenes.Despite its shortcomings, the movie did manage to deliver some captivating action sequences. The choreography and special effects were commendable, providing thrilling and visually engaging moments that momentarily grabbed my attention.It's worth noting that the film did touch upon relevant societal issues. It attempted to address thought-provoking themes, tackling subjects such as identity and the consequences of unchecked power.

Sadly, I was disappointed. The place is rather small and was packed by the time we came (about 10pm). It took ages to get a drink and it's hard to get around the bar with so many people standing around. That said, my drink was nice and strong and the prices are reasonable. nnNow the karaoke crowd was pretty fun.. . mostly hipsters with a few good singers but mostly people trying to be funny with their song choice. The karaoke host was just lame. He was an older guy, and seemed rather cranky and jaded, which really kills the mood when you're a karaoke buff looking for a good time. And he had a CD setup -- seriously, CDs, in 2010. His song list was waaaaaay out of date. He had a tiny TV screen that was setup in such a way that if you needed to look at the words, you would be facing away from the crowd. It was just really lackluster in my opinion.

The National shuttle driver was very nice and helpful. As soon as we got off the shuttle things turned unorganized. People wondering around the lot to pick out their cars.. .. . and many employees standing around smiling. .. . okay fine. nnWe picked out our car, which I admit is a nice feature of national. We drove to the exit where there are 3 lanes.. .. all three open.. .. NONE of them moving. Two lanes sat idle for 5 minutes.. .. .. idle as in standing still. Employees still standing around the lot smiling got to be a bit annoying when it was not clear what was going on. nnWhen we finally arrived at the booth to get checked out the lady seemed a bit delirious and confused. She would ask us questions that were what we should be asking her. nnI rent a car roughly once-per-month and have never run into a facility as poorly run as this.. ..

The produce is always fresh, and the meat and fish departments are excellent. The staff is friendly and helpful, and the prices are reasonable. The deli has a great selection of prepared foods, and the bakery has some of the best pastries in town. I would recommend it to anyone looking for a good grocery store. It’s also very convenient, with plenty of free parking and easy access to public transportation. It’s open late, so you can always find time to shop, even if you have a busy schedule. The store is also very clean and well-organized, so it’s easy to find what you’re looking for. The staff is always happy to help and very knowledgeable about the products. I would recommend it to anyone looking for a good grocery store. I’ve been shopping here for years and have always been satisfied with the service. The prices are also very reasonable, and the store often has sales and specials. I’ve been able to find some great deals on items I need. The store is clean, well-organized, and has a wide variety of products. The staff is always friendly and helpful, and they’re always willing to go the extra mile to make sure I have a good shopping experience. I’m so glad I found this store. It’s the perfect place to shop for groceries and other household items. I would recommend it to anyone looking for a good shopping experience.

Aveda salons, in my opinion, have a reputation of being soothing, presentable and tasteful.Aveda salons, in my opinion, have a reputation of being soothing, presentable and tasteful. It is no surprise that customers will pay high prices for such an experience. I recently visited the Aveda salon in downtown Seattle. The salon is located in a nice area and has a nice atmosphere. Upon entering, there is a reception area and a small waiting area with a few magazines and books to read. There is also a small retail area where you can purchase Aveda products.The receptionist greeted me and asked me if I had an appointment. I did not have an appointment and she informed me that they could take me in about 30 minutes. I did not mind waiting so I had a seat in the waiting area. After about 20 minutes the receptionist came back and took me to my stylist.The stylist introduced herself and asked me what I wanted to do with my hair. I told her that I wanted to get my hair cut and colored. She then asked me about my hair care routine and my lifestyle. She listened attentively and then made some recommendations for my hair type. I agreed with her recommendations and she got started on my hair.The stylist did an excellent job cutting and coloring my hair. She was very careful and made sure that I was happy with the results. I was very pleased with the final outcome and I felt that the price was reasonable for the level of service I received.Overall, I was very impressed with my experience at the Aveda salon. The staff was friendly and professional, the salon was clean and inviting, and the prices were reasonable. I would definitely recommend this salon to anyone looking for a high-quality hair care experience.

Tucked away in the heart of CMU, we recently discovered that our go-to spot for Indian cuisine is actually part of the renowned Taste of India chain. Boasting an extensive menu, this restaurant treats patrons to a culinary journey across India with dishes that showcase bold flavors, rich spices, and traditional cooking methods.Prices are exceedingly reasonable, especially when considering the generous portion sizes and premium ingredients used. With each visit, we've experienced warm and attentive service, including thoughtful explanations of each dish and unwavering attention to ensuring our needs are met.The ambiance is cozy and inviting, complete with traditional Indian decor and soft music that creates a tranquil atmosphere. The standout dish that keeps us coming back is the Chicken Tikka Masala - chicken marinated in a blend of spices and yogurt, grilled to perfection, and served in a velvety sauce with a subtle kick of spices. Equally delicious is the Palak Paneer, a creamy spinach dish boasting fresh paneer cheese and a harmonious blend of spices.Fresh, hot, and fluffy, their naan bread is the perfect accompaniment to their array of sauces and curries. Rice dishes are equally impressive, with aromatic blends of herbs and spices that enhance the overall flavor profile.We're thrilled to have discovered that our beloved Indian eatery is part of the esteemed Taste of India chain. We eagerly look forward to our next visit, where we'll indulge in more of their authentic and mouthwatering Indian cuisine.

Oh man, I would absolutely love to be invited on a date to this place! From the moment I stepped inside, I was captivated by the enchanting ambiance. The room was dimly lit, with soft, flickering candlelight that created an intimate atmosphere perfect for a romantic evening. The decor was tastefully done, blending modern elegance with rustic elements, resulting in a visually stunning setting. The staff were attentive and courteous, welcoming us with warm smiles that immediately made us feel comfortable and at ease.As we looked over the menu, my taste buds were already tingling with anticipation. The restaurant offered a diverse selection of dishes, each one more tantalizing than the next. From delectable appetizers to mouthwatering main courses, there was something to satisfy every palate. We decided to start our meal with the chef's special bruschetta, a delightful combination of fresh tomatoes, basil, and creamy mozzarella on toasted crusty bread. The explosion of flavors was truly divine, leaving us craving for more.For our main courses, we chose the restaurant's signature dish: the grilled salmon fillet. It was a true culinary masterpiece. The fish was cooked to perfection, tender and flaky, accompanied by a medley of roasted vegetables that provided a delightful crunch. The portions were generous, leaving us completely satisfied yet still wanting to savor every last bite. To complement our meal, we indulged in a bottle of the finest red wine from their extensive collection, which elevated the entire dining experience.However, the highlight of the evening was the impeccable service. The waitstaff were knowledgeable, providing detailed descriptions of each dish and eagerly catering to our preferences. Their promptness and attention to detail were unparalleled, ensuring that everything was to our liking throughout the evening. It is truly rare to find such a combination of exceptional service and exquisite cuisine in one place.

I just happen to browse online on priceline and got this place book. I was already happy at this point that I am staying at a Marriott because I have always had great experiences at all the Marriotts across the country, but what happens next makes me love this location even more. I am extremly happy I didnt let some of the previous reviews deter me from booking here. When i arrived, my check in process was easy, the front desk was friendly, and I was able to check in early (which I was on a 16 hour drive cross country so i was ready to rest). My room was clean, the bathroom is immaculate, and it had everything i needed. Even the smallest things such as hair dryer, ethernet wire, and the ginger orange smell hair products made this stay VERY pleasant. I wanted to bring my work computer up to my room and the front desk gentleman was very helpful in assisting me with all my questions and helping with the cart so i can take it upstairs. When I travel, I dont expect hotel staff to wait on my hand and feet and I am not a needy guest, as long as they are professional and willing to help when i ask for it, I am satisfied with my stay. I asked for a different coffee maker since it wasnt working and they brought me another right to my room within a few minutes, thats what i call attentive and prompt service. All in all, I highly recommend this Marriottt.

I liked the decor, with exposed brick walls, soft lighting and we went there on a night with live music. But the positives stop there. I had the veggie burger. The burger was the typical veggie burger that a lot of restaurants are trying now - with all of the veggies in the patty - and people continually make it mushy. The mushy texture made me nauseous so I didn't finish that. I got the side of garlic mashed potatoes, and I'm pretty confident those came out of a box, though that ended up being my favorite thing. nnMy date got nachos, which are advertised with lots of goodies on top. Lies! They were covered in melted but cold cheese, a sprinkle of diced tomatoes and a scoop each of guacamole and sour cream. Neither one of us ate more than 3 chips. nnFinally, I ordered Patron with Rose's line juice. First sip.. . Completely bitter and disgusting! So I asked her. And she said, oh right, we don't have sweetened line juice, he probably just used lime juice. So I asked for her to add some simple syrup, which she did. And then it was drinkable. But come on. She should have thought of that upfront! nnDon't go here for anything but the walls and music.

The food is always delicious and well prepared, and the service is excellent. The atmosphere is relaxed and friendly, and it is a great place to start the day. I have tried many different dishes here, and I have never been disappointed. There is something for everyone, from classic American breakfasts like pancakes and waffles to more unusual items like huevos rancheros and breakfast burritos. The portions are also very generous. And the prices are very reasonable, especially considering the quality of the food. I always leave feeling full and satisfied. The service is also very good. The servers are always friendly and attentive, and they make sure that you have everything you need. They are also very knowledgeable about the menu and are happy to make recommendations if you are undecided about what to order. The restaurant is decorated in a warm and inviting style, and the atmosphere is casual and relaxed. It is a great place to relax and enjoy a delicious breakfast. In short, I highly recommend this restaurant for breakfast. It is one of my favorite places to eat in town.

However, when I picked up my items, 2 of my shirts had yellow and brown stains all over to collar and top of my shirts, so I sent those back, and took home the rest of my items. When I got home, 2 pairs of pants had been shrunk (tighter through the leg, slightly shorter inseam). Both of these had been dry cleaned before (at another location) and did not shrink. 4 weeks later I am still waiting on my other shirts. I have called and was told that the stains cannot be removed, it must have been my cologne, and that I would have to take them back to the store I bought them from and claim a "manufacturer defect. " This is completely unacceptable. Neither shirt has been worn more than 7 or 8 times each, and both have been dry cleaned before, and I always wear cologne, and I have never had this problem. They refuse to do anything about it. I have been given the run around on the phone, but am basically being told it is my fault that they ruined my shirts.

With, well, a catch. nnIt's the flagship store for the Giant Eagle chain, and they're strongly competing against the Whole Foods and Trader Joe's, both less than a mile away. They stock a large number of quirky items you normally would only see at specialty stores, and the produce section is amazing. The employees in the produce section are also amazing, as many of them can spout off a recipe for any particular item they sell, with a story and a smile. nnThat said, the traffic in and out of this place is pretty rough, and the prices on random items are much higher than you might expect, while the average price of items are actually pretty darn good. Hummus is ridiculously expensive. Juice is cheap. Dunno. nnEvery time we go here, we come out highly stressed, which sucks. The parking lot, traffic, and lines all make for a bad time, so I shop elsewhere unless I really need something that other places don't stock.

I decided to give it a try. From the moment I walked in, I was greeted by a warm and welcoming atmosphere. The modern decor and cozy seating arrangements set the perfect tone for a relaxed dining experience. The menu at Ds was diverse and offered a wide range of choices to suit different tastes. Being a fan of fish, I opted for the grilled salmon, which did not disappoint. The fish was perfectly cooked, moist and flaky, with a delightful smoky flavor. The salmon was served with a refreshing side salad dressed in a tangy vinaigrette. The service at Ds was impeccable. The attentive staff was knowledgeable about the menu and offered excellent recommendations. The food was served promptly, and my water glass was never empty. One thing that stood out at Ds was the attention to detail. The food was beautifully presented, showcasing the chef’s artistic flair. The portions were generous, ensuring that no one left the table hungry. The cocktail menu featured both creative concoctions and classic favorites. I indulged in a Ds martini, which was expertly crafted and packed a flavorful punch. I can say with confidence that Ds lives up to its reputation and is a must-visit for anyone in town looking for a superb dining experience. What made my visit to Ds so memorable was the overall dining experience. The ambiance, the delicious food, the attentive service—everything came together to create a memorable dining experience. I would recommend Ds to anyone looking for a wonderful meal in a warm and welcoming atmosphere. Whether you’re a local or just passing through, I highly recommend giving Ds a try.

I also should have known that I shouldn't have cheated on my diet. nnI figured I'd choose today to be my cheat day and order with everyone else at work for take out. 'nnI'm regretting this decision now as I am sitting here in discomfort. Not sure what on the salad made me feel this way. But I'm not liking the effect I'm getting right now. I love animals, and I have rescued 5 dogs and cats. Every time I go to Stoney Creek, they try to force me to buy exorbitantly priced tests and medications. nnDr. Jill Pascarella is rude, condescending, and lacks any empathy for her clients. Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review in a human style:"So, I decided to remedy that situation by ordering the 'Grease-Fest Combo' at the local diner. It came with a side of gravy-smothered fries, a 'juicy' burger drenched in cheese sauce, and a milkshake that could double as a motor oil substitute. I was excited to indulge in some heart attack-inducing goodness, but unfortunately, the meal was a letdown.The fries were limp and flavorless, and the burger was cooked to a dry, overcooked mess. The cheese sauce was watery and lacked any discernible flavor. The milkshake tasted like a mix of stale ice cream and gasoline. Maybe they should reconsider the name of their combo meal, because it certainly didn't live up to its promise."

The long corridor provides ample space for guests to mingle and move about, while the stage and sound system at the far end create a focal point for live performances and presentations. The lighting system is state-of-the-art, with a range of options to suit different types of events. The venue’s minimalist design allows for maximum creativity and flexibility. The stage is spacious enough to accommodate a variety of acts, from solo musicians to full bands and even dance performances. The sound system is also top-notch, ensuring that music and speeches are crystal clear and well-balanced. The versatility of The Artworks is one of its most notable features. The venue can be easily transformed to suit a wide range of events, from weddings and private parties to art exhibitions and live concerts. The Artworks’ convenient location is another major plus, with easy access to public transport and a host of nearby amenities. The staff are also extremely helpful and accommodating, working closely with hosts to ensure that every detail is taken care of. The venue is also wheelchair accessible, ensuring that everyone can enjoy the space. There is a nearby car park for guests who prefer to drive. The Artworks is a unique event space that offers a blank canvas for hosts to create their own unforgettable experience.

I've got to say, I really REALLY love this place! nnI had a really awesome vet in my home town, so naturally I was worried about bringing my animals to see a new one (we had one REALLY bad vet once upon a time). A friend recommended this place to me, so I decided to try it out. nnOn the first visit, they quickly got us into an exam room with very minimal wait. The vet tech came very quickly, and she was very good to my dog. Then I met Dr. Hodges. She is SUCH an amazing vet! She was so good with both my animals and me. She does a great job of answering questions, and she's really into alternative medicine for animals. While I was spazzing out because my dog was sick, she really helped to assuage my concerns. nnOne time I was able to bring my dog immediately in for an emergency appointment. It ended up being nothing serious, but she personally saw him about 5 minutes after we arrived. The emergency visit was the same price as the regular office visit (although it was probably because it was during hours). nnOne of my other favorite things is that Dr. Hodges calls me personally with the results of any testing and to check up on the animals after they've had an appointment. It reminds me of my previous vet, and I really like that she gives great, practical, and easy-to-understand information. nnThe only complaint I have is that the prices are a little high (it's $58 for an office visit). They might just be the standard cost for a vet visit here in Charlotte, but I have no idea since this is the only vet I've used here. Nevertheless, they take such good care of my boys, it's totally worth it. They also participate in a lot of community events with animals, and I really love that about them as well. nnI totally, highly recommend this vet if you are in the market for one! !

It's a shame it's the only real photography store in the city, but they could definitley do better. nnAs part of a black and white photography class, I needed to pick up film and photo paper, among a few other things. While Bernie's had everything I needed (and lots of other supplies) I didn't feel all that appreciated for coming in. I spent a couple hundred bucks and felt like I was being shuffled out the door as quickly as possible. nnI will give the one guy at the counter some credit because a woman right before me asked to get a roll or two of film developed in just barely an hour. I could tell he wanted to get to it quickly, but still felt like I was hassling the guy just being there. nnI hate to see real camera stores shut down, but unless I'm in a pinch I'm probably going to order supplies online from now on.

Sure, here's a polished version of the review:I recently had the misfortune of dining at [Restaurant Name], and I must say that it was a thoroughly disappointing experience. **Here is the polished review**:The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with soft music and dim lighting. The knowledgeable and friendly staff are always happy to provide recommendations. The extensive wine list boasts a wide variety of wines from around the world, and the impressive food menu offers a range of small plates and entrees.I began with a glass of the 2010 Barolo, recommended by our server. Its beautiful, deep garnet color was accompanied by complex aromas of cherry, plum, and spice. For my meal, I ordered the roasted beet salad and the grilled salmon. The delicious salad combined roasted beets, goat cheese, and walnuts. The perfectly cooked salmon was served with a lemon-herb sauce.I finished with the divine chocolate torte, a rich and decadent dessert that was the perfect ending to a wonderful meal.Overall, I highly recommend Barrique. It offers a great atmosphere, friendly and knowledgeable staff, and excellent food and wine.If you are looking for a great wine bar in Milwaukee, I highly recommend Barrique.

Despite their appearance, each cake was also slightly dry. The devil's food cake was so dry that it tasted stale. Even with a glass of milk, the dryness of the cake was overwhelming. Although the carrot cake appeared moist, it also lacked flavor. The frosting on each cake tasted like standard grocery-store frosting and was overly sweet. The chocolate frosting on the devil's food cake tasted slightly better than the cream cheese frosting on the carrot cake or the vanilla frosting on the red velvet cake, but none of the frostings were remarkable. There wasn't much difference between the cakes; they all tasted similar. To be fair, the red velvet cake had a slightly better flavor than the other two cakes, but all three lacked quality ingredients. The red velvet cake was slightly less dry than the other two cakes, but its cream cheese frosting was overly sweet. The carrot cake, which looked very moist, tasted bland and had a gritty texture. Even though the devil's food cake had a good chocolate flavor, it was dry and stale.

Desperation drives most of my visits, I suppose, and this one was no different. The Cross Plains State Bank (located just down the road from here) has recently closed, and I was in need of cash prior to my hair cut. I figured Walgreens would have an ATM, or at the very least, would provide cash back with a purchase. nn- ATM (from BMO Harris) is about 91 years old, charges a $3 fee, and is slower than molasses. Worse yet, after staring at the screen as it read "Transaction Processing" for 6-minutes, the ATM failed to work. It spit out a receipt that said, "Transaction Failed. " Be warned - the service fee is an unreasonable $3; however, since I doubt this thing ever works, you won't pay the fee (you also won't get any cash). nn- Prices are outrageous. With the ATM down, I made my way to the soda area so that I could buy a Diet Coke and then get cash back from the register. Their soda prices are a good $0. 30 higher than anyone else. I shouldn't be surprised, nor should I be complaining about this, as it's no secret that Walgreens generally charges a premium for anything in their store. nn- Checkouts are slow. I got into line to check-out with my Diet Coke. I was 7th in line. One cashier. After 5 people were checked out (only one more person in front of me), the cashier fires-up the microphone and asks for more checkout assistance. Ugh. nn- When I went to check out and hit the "cash back" option, the kid who was running the newly opened register informed me that he didn't have $20 in his drawer to give me for change. Really? He had to get a $20 from the other checker. nn- You can't enter/exit from University; you have to enter from Branch Streetnn+ At least it's bright in here and the store feels fairly clean.

nnMy cat has cancer and was experiencing some new symptoms that needed possible ultrasounds. We came to this CVS location because it was after hours and the CVS I usually go to in Matthews wasn't going to have the ultrasound tech as soon as needed, so we drove 30 minutes in the opposite direction to this location in Huntersville. nnWhen we walked in around 10PM on a Friday, the place was packed to the brim. All the exam rooms were full and there were about 10 people in the lobby. Okay, so maybe that night we just had bad luck? nnNo. Our greeting at the front desk was awful. The secretary didn't even make eye contact with us and acted like we were inconveniencing her. She was appalling and rude. nnWe were told we would have to wait at least an hour because they were overly busy, which we understood. After a couple hours, we asked if we could just check our sick kitty into the ICU for the night and leave because we knew she needed overnight supervision. Well, the secretary didn't even come relay the NO response to us. nnLuckily, not all of the staff were as dreadful as the secretary. But we ended up waiting over 5 hours (until 3: 30AM), only to be told that they were admitting her into the ICU overnight and running tests (exactly as we requested before). I know the protocol was to have the doctor chat a moment in person before we left, but we felt that common sense wasn't being put to use at this facility.

We were seated right away, and given 2 large baskets of tortilla chips for our party of 5. I really like how they give us our own bowl of salsa per person, double dipping time! The restaurant wasn't too crowded, so service was fairly good. I had just landed from SFO, so wanted to unwind with my jack and coke. Others ordered the huge frozen margaritas that were very tasty! Very addicting! nnWe ordered guacamole, which came in a huge molcajete, those stone looking bowls. Lots of guac but a bit bland for my taste, had to add salt to my portion. nnFlipped through their book of a menu, and everything is fairly cheap! I wanted variety so went for the chicken tamale, ground pork enchilada, and chile releno with refried beans and mexican rice. All this for $10! And the plate was huge!! ! The enchilada was bleh, tamale was decent, but was blown away that their chile releno was so tasty. It's really hard to find a good one, but this chile not only had a kick to it, but lots of cheese. nnCoworkers also all enjoyed their dishes for the most part. The food is good, but not out of this world. Towards the end of dinner, service was.. . gone. I waited 10 minutes for another jack and coke, and he totally forgot so had to ask another guy. It basically came as my dessert as we were all done. They do give a mini dessert to all, fried crispers with whipped cream on top and a bunch of cinnamon. nnDecent tasting food and huge portions for a very low price.
